session_title: Academic Entrepreneurship and Getting Discoveries into Patients, part one: Liftoff
talk_title: Building the Bridge from Academia to Clinic
mechanistic_blindspot: The speaker mentions the company has had 'ups and downs' and a significant reduction in staff (60 to 12), suggesting instability in the asset's development or funding.
crispro_top_description: The speaker's focus on NK cells and the transition to in vivo CAR-NK suggests a need for better targeting and delivery mechanisms.

talk_title: Journey from academia to pharma and lessons on entrepreneurship
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "When one has an idea, whether it's a therapeutic or a diagnostic, and you're thinking about pitching the idea, it's often helpful to understand what is in the mind of the venture capitalist." | Speaker said: "We knew upfront that we wanted to move to commercialization because we knew that you could do the approach in academia. That was happening already."
crispro_top_description: Foundation Medicine’s pan‑cancer NGS platform lacks a defined biomarker‑driven use case for early‑stage disease, creating an opening for CrisPRO to provide predictive biomarkers that de‑risk broad diagnostic programs.

talk_title: From academic discovery to venture funding: lessons from a lung‑cancer diagnostic
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I remember looking at my colleague from BU and realizing we hadn't really thought deeply about it. And I was much younger back then, so when I didn't know the answer to the question, I kept quiet, but my colleague didn't, and he gave the famous last words, which were, 'Well, how much money do you have?'" | Speaker said: "We raised a B round. It was extremely painful. We got it done."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed a detailed 80‑gene panel (observation) but concluded that the test needed a clear intended use population (contradiction) – indicating a mismatch between assay complexity and market strategy.
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of a narrowly defined intended use population for early‑lung cancer detection
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can refine the 80‑gene panel into a minimal, AI‑derived signature linked to a high‑risk population, improving regulatory and payer acceptance.

talk_title: Translating cell therapy for HPV‑associated cancers from academia to commercial development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "It does require substantial infrastructure and know‑how at your institution. Either you need to know how to do it, or you need a mentor who knows how to do it, or you need an institution that can develop these capabilities at a leadership level." | Speaker said: "I think one of the very powerful things about being an academic is there are a lot of resources there. There are a lot of things that academic centers can do. You also have an advantage in drug development from a regulatory standpoint. My interactions with the FDA as an academic are much, much easier than those for anyone at J&J."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted the ease of FDA interaction for academics (observation) but also said “you can propose it and file an IND without a pre‑IND meeting, which puts pressure on the FDA” (contradiction) – indicating a tension between perceived regulatory flexibility and actual procedural risk.
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited understanding of tumor microenvironment factors that may suppress infused T cells
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can analyze transcriptomic and spatial data from treated lesions to identify immune evasion pathways for combinatorial targeting.

talk_title: Resilience and the “Catch‑2022” in early‑stage biotech
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Without money, you can't have team, and if you don't have a team, the VCs cannot come. So I will finish with that. Resilience, patient, and if you are convinced, maybe you can succeed, but with difficulties." | Speaker said: "When you deal with investors, they present you a junior analyst who don't know your target, don't know the science, how to deal with such VCs."
mechanistic_blindspot: No clear preclinical justification for combining MEK inhibitor with KRAS‑G12C inhibitor in metastatic setting
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model pathway interactions to prioritize rational combinations and identify biomarkers for patient selection.

institution: University of Utah, Huntsman Cancer Institute
talk_title: Community-engaged research partnerships
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Folks will partner with you if you address their priorities." | Speaker said: "We brought in about $10 million to the state to help address tobacco control." | Speaker said: "We enrolled 12,000 patients in a tobacco cessation study when we hoped for 6,000." | Speaker said: "If your funding runs out, you need to still be somehow in there working with them."
crispro_top_description: Lack of readiness-to-quit biomarker could be addressed by CrisPRO predictive modeling to target high‑likelihood quitters.

talk_title: Social capital interventions in persistent poverty neighborhoods
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We chose four particular neighborhoods ... to focus on social capital." | Speaker said: "We are implementing Cares For You ... a cancer risk education curriculum for middle schoolers." | Speaker said: "Bridge to Quit ... aims to increase patient portal uptake and smoking cessation." | Speaker said: "We are training a Career Enhancement Corps to develop community‑based research coordinators."
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could provide digital literacy scoring and predictive modeling to enhance Bridge to Quit enrollment strategies.

talk_title: Upstream interventions: guaranteed income and EITC pilots
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The guaranteed income program was run through these different organizations across the state... 3,000 participants that were randomized to treatment or control." | Speaker said: "Our hypothesis here was the guaranteed income reduces stress and promotes healthier behaviors." | Speaker said: "We are looking at factors related to colon cancer, looking at diet and other types of factors." | Speaker said: "We have 11 different pilot grants, 50% of awardees have submitted further proposals, 13% secured additional funding."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that guaranteed income improves stress and diet (observation) but concluded the study is primarily about cancer outcomes without presenting cancer incidence data — ignoring the gap between upstream behavior change and downstream cancer metrics.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential lack of direct linkage between income boost and measurable cancer‑specific outcomes
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model how income changes translate into measurable dietary and screening behavior changes, linking upstream cash to downstream cancer risk.

talk_title: Iterative engineering of GPCR-targeted synthetic TCRs and antigen receptor STAR cells in advanced solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the persistence is limited" | Speaker said: "we only enrolled three patients" | Speaker said: "we switched into membrane-bound IL-15"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that only responders had massive STAR T expansion, then concluded that the iterative design "solved the initial expansion hurdle" while persistence remained limited
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited in vivo persistence of engineered cells despite IL-15 armoring
crispro_top_description: Identify patients with high tumor CXCL1/2/5/8 chemokine expression to match CXCR2‑armed STAR T cells

talk_title: Cellular Architecture of Infused TILs Predicts Efficacy of Autologous TIL Therapy in Acral Melanoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the production quality is more important than the quantity" | Speaker said: "the durable immunity comes from the progenitor exhaust T cells"
mechanistic_blindspot: Low proportion of progenitor exhausted T cells leads to limited durability
crispro_top_description: Develop a predictive signature (TFH + intermediate exhausted T‑cell markers) to select patients for TIL therapy

institution: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
talk_title: First‑in‑class chimeric antigen receptor dendritic cell platform driving broad and durable antitumor immunity in solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "CAR DCs induce a strong costimulatory program across many different costimulatory molecules" | Speaker said: "30 out of 30 mice showed complete rejection"
crispro_top_description: Pair CAR‑DCs with checkpoint blockade to sustain T‑cell activation after DC‑mediated priming

talk_title: Decoding the Medulloblastoma Serpinosome Prioritizes the Oncofetal Antigen GPC2 for Potent CAR T Cell Therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "GPC2 is the top candidate and outperforms other antigens" | Speaker said: "c‑Jun overexpression mitigates antigen‑low tumor escape"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that GPC2 density is modest yet concluded that the CAR T product is broadly effective without extensive biomarker gating
failing_target: GPC2‑CAR T cells without c‑Jun augmentation
mechanistic_blindspot: Tumor antigen down‑regulation leading to escape
crispro_top_description: Define a quantitative GPC2 density cutoff (>6,000 molecules/cell) for patient selection
cited_competitors: GD2 CAR T programs; B7‑H3 CAR T programs

talk_title: NK TCR cells, a next generation platform expanding NK cell therapeutic potential
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "NK TCR cells kill two tumors in less than two minutes" | Speaker said: "no CRS, ICANS or GvHD observed in patient"
failing_target: NK TCR platform without MHC‑I dependence
mechanistic_blindspot: Reliance on tumor MHC‑I expression for TCR recognition may limit activity against MHC‑I‑low tumors
crispro_top_description: Pair NK TCR cells with agents that up‑regulate MHC‑I (e.g., IFN‑γ) to broaden targetability

talk_title: Universal T cell therapy screening library and preclinical models of CAR T cell therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "71% of hits are matching in the same direction between screens and model" | Speaker said: "CACTUS library contains 636 members"
crispro_top_description: Leverage CrisPRO to prioritize combinatorial gene edits that improve CAR T persistence and reduce exhaustion

talk_title: Triple Disruption of CCDC6, ZEB1B, and ASB2 Promotes the Therapeutic Efficacy of Glypican‑3 Targeted T Cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the majority of this response was a clonal expansion" | Speaker said: "triple knockout cells showed complete tumor control and survival"
failing_target: Standard GPC3‑CAR T (IL‑15 armored) without gene disruption
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of disruption of negative regulators (CBLB, CCDC6, ASB2) limits clonal expansion
crispro_top_description: Introduce CRISPR‑mediated knockout of CBLB, CCDC6, and ASB2 into GPC3‑CAR manufacturing to recapitulate patient clone expansion

session_title: Advanced Antibody Conjugate and Targeted Therapeutic Platforms
talk_title: The SynthBody Platform: A multifunctional, multivalent, multispecific antibody drug conjugate platform
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'we use this as essentially our North Star' | Speaker said: 'if you're going to only pay attention to a single slide in the entire talk, this is the one to pay attention to' | Speaker said: 'we can make synbodies that have absolutely terrible PK, but we've also learned to make these molecules with really excellent PK'
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of high-density antigen expression (unlike HER2) and failure to utilize cooperative binding to increase internalization and payload delivery
crispro_top_description: Developing multi-tiered logic-gated ADCs to simulate 'HER2-high' expression levels on targets with lower density

session_title: Advanced Antibody Conjugate and Targeted Therapeutic Platforms
talk_title: M7437: A novel anti-LY6E ADC using a Top1 inhibitor payload
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'we may not have to rely on a biomarker-based patient stratification or selection' | Speaker said: 'it's a bit difficult to discriminate how much would be added on from the LY6E expression' (regarding bone marrow tox)
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential for target-mediated drug disposition (TMDD) due to LY6E expression on NK and B cells
crispro_top_description: Identifying the exact threshold of LY6E expression required for efficacy to avoid 'unselected' trial failure
cited_competitors: 9b12 MMAE; DATO-DXd; Rina-S; Ralu-DXd; Enhertu / T-DXd

session_title: Advanced Antibody Conjugate and Targeted Therapeutic Platforms
talk_title: Preclinical evaluation of target-mediated clearance and alternative payloads in cadherin 17-directed antibody drug conjugates
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'we got an early signal in patients that we started to see higher than expected... clearance' | Speaker said: 'unfortunately, we saw the exact same effect' (regarding payload switch not fixing PK)
mechanistic_blindspot: Target-mediated clearance (TMC) due to high expression of Cadherin 17 in normal colon and small intestine (antigen sink)
crispro_top_description: Overcoming P-gp mediated resistance in CRC by switching to Top1 inhibitors (exatecan)

session_title: Advanced Antibody Conjugate and Targeted Therapeutic Platforms
talk_title: Nuzepha (nuzephatide pepidotin): A bicycle drug conjugate targeting EphA2
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'the preliminary safety profile is completely differentiated from previous FA2 targeting conjugates' | Speaker said: 'I think that the demonstration is that half-life is adequate'
crispro_top_description: Using small bicyclic peptides to achieve rapid tumor penetration and fast renal clearance to widen the therapeutic window

talk_title: Phase one trial of intraperitoneal mesothelin-targeted CAR T therapy in patients with mesothelin-positive esophageal and gastric cancers with peritoneal carcinomatosis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "intraperitoneal mesothelin-targeted CAR T cell administration was safe" | Speaker said: "Antitumor activity ... was limited, and this is likely reflecting the heterogeneous target expression" | Speaker said: "Given the safety data that we observed and the higher level of mesothelin expression in peritoneal mesothelioma, we are amending the study to recruit such patients in future"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed CAR‑T persistence beyond day 100 in peripheral blood, then concluded that antitumor activity was limited – ignoring the persistence data that might support efficacy
mechanistic_blindspot: heterogeneous mesothelin expression in esophageal/gastric adenocarcinoma leading to limited tumor cell targeting
crispro_top_description: Need for quantitative mesothelin expression or alternative targets to overcome heterogeneous antigen loss

talk_title: Safety and preliminary efficacy of a novel Lego‑STIM armored mesothelin‑targeted CAR T therapy in advanced solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "UCMYM802 is a CAR T product prepared by MRA electroporation protocol process" | Speaker said: "The most common treatment‑related adverse events ... were cytokine release syndrome, fever, decreased lymphocyte count, anemia, prolonged prothrombin time, and hypothermia" | Speaker said: "One patient experienced a grade four cytokine release syndrome ... defined as DLT" | Speaker said: "Objective response rate was 28.6% and disease control rate was 57.1%"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted rapid, transient CAR expression (peak 1 h, decline after 8 h) yet concluded that the product demonstrates a durable efficacy signal
mechanistic_blindspot: High-dose (1 billion cells) associated with grade 4 CRS and severe cytokine‑mediated toxicities
crispro_top_description: Identify mesothelin‑low or negative clones driving resistance to the CAR T product

talk_title: First‑in‑human Phase I study of KSQ001EX, a CRISPR‑edited SOX1‑inactivated TIL therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "KSQ001EX is an engineered TIL cell therapy using CRISPR‑Cas9 editing to remove the gene SOX1" | Speaker said: "The regimen was well‑tolerated with no high‑grade dose limiting toxicities" | Speaker said: "We see dose‑dependent target lesion reductions with better efficacy in patients treated above the median cell dose" | Speaker said: "All patients experienced some tumor reduction with an ORR of 33%"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented robust peripheral expansion of edited TIL (up to 500 days) yet admitted that two patients could not be treated due to manufacturing failures
mechanistic_blindspot: Patients with low baseline TIL yield (<1 billion cells) may not achieve sufficient effector numbers
crispro_top_description: Rescue low‑yield TIL manufacturing by integrating CrisPRO‑guided culture conditions to boost expansion

talk_title: Five‑year survival of Tebentafusp in previously treated metastatic uveal melanoma (Phase III)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Hazard ratio for overall survival is 0.67" | Speaker said: "At the five‑year landmark, there's a doubling of overall survival from 8% to 16%" | Speaker said: "ctDNA clearance at week nine correlated with a hazard ratio of 0.32" | Speaker said: "Patients treated beyond radiographic progression had longer post‑progression survival"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented modest RECIST response rates yet emphasized a strong overall survival benefit, indicating a disconnect between radiographic and survival outcomes
mechanistic_blindspot: Therapy requires HLA‑A0201 presentation; patients lacking this allele are excluded, limiting broader applicability
crispro_top_description: Develop a combined HLA‑A0201 and ctDNA baseline model to identify patients most likely to benefit

talk_title: Phase 1b/2a study of RZ001 (adenovirus‑mediated HTERT trans‑splicing ribozyme) with velganciclovir and atezolizumab/bevacizumab in HCC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "RZ001 is an adenovirus‑mediated transsplicing ribozyme targeting and replacing HTERT mRNA" | Speaker said: "Objective response was about 40% by RECIST 1.1 or 61.5% by modified RECIST" | Speaker said: "All grade 3 or higher TAEs were observed in 53% of patients, but no grade 4 or 5 TLAs occurred" | Speaker said: "No dose‑limiting toxicity was observed"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized a favorable safety profile (no DLT) while reporting >50% grade 3 adverse events
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on normal hepatocytes despite claimed tumor selectivity
crispro_top_description: Add a randomized atezolizumab/bevacizumab control to quantify RZ001 added benefit

talk_title: Phase 1/2 trial of LOAD‑703 (adenovirus‑CD40L/4‑1BBL) with nab‑paclitaxel, gemcitabine, and atezolizumab in advanced pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Overall response rate in arm two was 38% across dose levels, with three complete responses" | Speaker said: "Patients treated first line had median overall survival of 14.7 months and ORR 54%" | Speaker said: "We observed massive upregulation of adaptive immune biomarkers and down‑regulation of fibroblast proliferation markers" | Speaker said: "Safety profile was favorable; most AEs were related to chemotherapy"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted robust immune activation biomarkers yet concluded that the exact contribution of LOAD‑703 versus atezolizumab remains unknown
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited activity in later‑line patients; benefit appears confined to first‑line setting
crispro_top_description: Implement a four‑arm randomized study to dissect contributions of chemotherapy, atezolizumab, and LOAD‑703

talk_title: ABO2203: mRNA‑encoded CD3‑CD19 T cell engager for relapsed/refractory B‑cell NHL
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "ABO2203 demonstrated a more favorable pharmacokinetics, improved safety, and enhanced antitumor potency compared to protein‑based TCE" | Speaker said: "Overall response rate across all cohorts was 67% and DCR 78%" | Speaker said: "Four patients achieved complete remission, including three at high dose" | Speaker said: "No dose‑limiting toxicities, no CRS, no ICANS, and only low‑grade pyrexia"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized low cytokine release and absence of CRS yet described fever and transient IL‑6 spikes, which are typical CRS features
mechanistic_blindspot: Patients with CD19‑negative relapse (post‑CAR‑T) may not respond; no data yet on CD19 loss
crispro_top_description: Develop next‑generation mRNA TCEs targeting CD20 or CD22 for CD19‑negative relapses

talk_title: Risk stratification and management of non‑muscle invasive bladder cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have a new framework for designing clinical trials and what control arms are needed, if any." | Speaker noted: "These therapies are all very expensive... $20 million for one additional life year." | Speaker admitted: "We still probably need help on how to best deploy those for patients that are in front of us."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high 53%–71% complete response rates for intravesical agents, then concluded that "we still probably need help on how to best deploy those for patients" – ignoring the strong efficacy data.
failing_target: Intravesical therapies (e.g., Valrubicin, Adstiladrin, Anctiva, TAR200)
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of molecular biomarker–guided patient selection for intravesical agents
crispro_top_description: Lack of molecular subtyping to select NMIBC patients who will benefit from BCG + IO combinations.

institution: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
talk_title: Precision biomarkers and adaptive therapy in muscle‑invasive bladder cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We need two sets of biomarkers to answer these questions" referring to adjuvant ctDNA and drug‑specific response biomarkers. | Speaker admitted: "The benefit of nivolumab in the adjuvant setting is limited by the challenges of adjuvant therapy for solid tumors in general". | Speaker noted: "We think that a subset of patients can be cured with TURBT plus systemic therapy, but of course, we need to do a little bit better here."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented a 57% pathologic CR rate with EV‑pembro and then said "we still need better biomarkers" despite the clear correlation between path CR and survival.
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of ctDNA‑guided patient selection leading to inclusion of ctDNA‑negative patients who do not benefit
crispro_top_description: A3A‑driven DASP state confers multi‑drug resistance via SOX2 up‑regulation.

talk_title: Modeling therapy resistance and rational sequential combinations in bladder cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We think that this state called DASP provides a new mechanistic angle for understanding how transient genotoxic stress is converted into heritable long lasting effects." | Speaker admitted: "We initially hypothesized that targeted drugs would be more tractable, but the phenotype turned out to be truly multi‑drug resistant." | Speaker noted: "These data support a mechanistic rationale for testing sequential CDK4/6 inhibitors with ADCs in EV‑resistant urothelial cancer."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that A3A exposure creates a multi‑drug resistant state, yet earlier claimed that "targeted drugs are more tractable" suggesting a contradiction between the model’s broad resistance and the focus on targeted agents.
mechanistic_blindspot: APOBEC3A‑driven FOXM1‑SOX2 reprogramming not addressed by current CDK4/6 inhibitors
crispro_top_description: Targeting the FOXM1‑SOX2 axis in APOBEC‑high tumors to overcome multi‑drug resistance.

talk_title: Chromatin remodeler CHD7 drives lineage plasticity and modulates replication competence in neuroendocrine prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we haven't defined the specific upstream regulator for CHD7" | Speaker said: "we do have plenty results to suggest that they're not directly regulated by AR"
crispro_top_description: CHD7 expression defines a subset of NEPC patients with ATR‑checkpoint dependence, but no clinical assay exists.

talk_title: Essential role of KDM5B‑SOX9 signaling in therapy‑induced neuroendocrine prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't have directly evidence to confirm SOX9 really contribute" | Speaker said: "the incidence of NEPC is only 15% to 5% around the world"
crispro_top_description: KDM5B inhibitors under development could be repurposed for NEPC if biomarker (KDM5B high / SOX9 activation) stratification is applied.

talk_title: Cooperation of DNMT and EZH2 in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the regulation or perturbing the regulators of these repressive mechanisms do differ functionally specific to the disease state" | Speaker said: "we haven't done it in the natural condition of NEPC development"
crispro_top_description: Combine EZH2 inhibitors with DNMT1 degraders to exploit compensatory epigenetic rewiring in NEPC.

talk_title: Targeting the ubiquitin reading function of DNMT1 as a next generation approach for epigenetic cancer therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we are still trying to refine these binders" | Speaker said: "these are not replication‑dependent"
crispro_top_description: UIM‑targeting offers a replication‑independent DNMT1 inhibition strategy that could overcome limitations of 5‑azacytidine.

talk_title: KDM2A driven epigenetic reprogramming promotes Barrett esophagus to adenocarcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "KDM2A was found to be increased and had a trend, but not very significant" | Speaker said: "we are still working on CUT&RUN data to show direct binding"
crispro_top_description: Existing KDM2A inhibitors could be repurposed for Barrett‑esophagus patients with high KDM2A expression.

talk_title: KMT2D is a master epigenetic regulator of lineage plasticity in lung squamous cell carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we did not actually see a significant difference at first for the overall survival" | Speaker said: "the catalytic region is important because that's the site where the protein does the enhancer regulator work"
crispro_top_description: Targeting the KMT2D‑FOXA1‑SOX2 enhancer axis could provide a more precise approach than pan‑H3K4 inhibitors.

talk_title: Asymmetric Emergence of Cardiovascular Disease in Hematologic Malignancy Survivors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have a shocking increase of CVD incidence, especially during the first two years of a hematological malignancy cancer diagnosis" | Speaker admitted: "I haven't looked into biochemical profiles or electrolyte changes yet"
crispro_top_description: Need for prospective biomarker-driven validation of proteasome inhibitor–induced cardiotoxicity in hematologic cancer survivors

talk_title: Decoding Melanoma Immunogenicity: A Comprehensive Proteogenomic, Immunopeptidomic, and Metabolomic Atlas
rhetorical_signals: Speaker noted: "a large percentage of those tumor antigen candidates were not recorded in the existing IEDB database" | Speaker admitted: "we do not see significant differences between the primary tumors versus the metastasis tumors in terms of the DNA alteration pattern"
crispro_top_description: Targeting the interferon‑UB subtype with high ubiquitination of DHX9 and AKT1 to overcome poor survival in melanoma

talk_title: Endocrine Therapy Adherence and 10‑Year Risk of Cardiometabolic Outcomes Among Women with Hormone Receptor‑Positive Breast Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "longer adherence to endocrine therapy was associated with greater risk of hypertension" | Speaker admitted: "we control for healthcare access at baseline but cannot fully control for follow‑up screening frequency"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AI use increases hypertension risk, yet concluded that both AI and tamoxifen should be maintained for survival benefit, ignoring the cardiovascular risk disparity
mechanistic_blindspot: Increased cardiometabolic toxicity with prolonged exposure not mitigated by current monitoring
crispro_top_description: Identify cardioprotective agents (e.g., antihypertensives) that can be co‑prescribed with aromatase inhibitors to mitigate metabolic risk

talk_title: Comprehensive Phenotypic Mapping of ALT Regulation Expands the Landscape of Targetable Suppressors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we found that proteasome inhibition leads to accumulation of PML protein and hyper‑ALT phenotype" | Speaker admitted: "we still need to test this in neuroblastoma, glioma, and PANETs"
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target accumulation of PML leading to hyper‑ALT‑driven cell death in normal tissues
crispro_top_description: Repurpose proteasome inhibitors for ALT‑positive cancers by combining with PML‑stabilizing agents to achieve selective hyper‑ALT lethality

talk_title: Protein Complex Remodeling in Metastatic Breast Cancer Cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we identified over 100 protein complexes, almost half of which are novel" | Speaker admitted: "we still need to link dynamic complexes to patient survival outcomes"
crispro_top_description: Target stress‑response assembly (gained after vernalstat) to sensitize TNBC to chemotherapy

talk_title: ASP2998‑TROP2 Targeted Immunomodulatory Antibody Drug Conjugate with Dual Payloads
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the STING agonist component leads to durable tumor immunity and re‑challenge protection" | Speaker admitted: "we have not set a Trop‑2 expression threshold in the first‑in‑human trial"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented strong preclinical efficacy of the STING‑containing ADC yet acknowledged that the trial does not yet stratify patients by Trop‑2 level, potentially contradicting the need for target expression
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects in normal epithelial tissues expressing Trop‑2 (cornea, kidney, urethra)
crispro_top_description: Develop a quantitative Trop‑2 expression assay to enrich for responders to ASP2998

talk_title: Targeting EYA4 Disrupts Genome Stability in Triple‑Negative Breast Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "EYA4 loss induces HR deficiency and sensitizes TNBC cells to DNA‑damaging agents" | Speaker admitted: "we have not yet tested the inhibitor in patient samples"
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory phosphatases may rescue HR function, limiting monotherapy activity
crispro_top_description: Combine EYA4 inhibition with PARP inhibitors to exploit induced HR deficiency in TNBC

talk_title: Neural integration of glioblastoma and therapeutic disconnect
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: perampanel is a drug that needs very careful titration because obviously you suffer from neuropsychiatric side effects. | Speaker said: we also need to develop bespoke translation readouts for neuronal connectivity in glioblastoma patients.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed electron‑microscopy evidence of bona‑fide synapses between neurons and tumor cells, then concluded that perampanel can be used broadly despite acknowledging neuropsychiatric toxicity that may limit dosing
mechanistic_blindspot: Off‑target CNS neuropsychiatric effects not mitigated by tumor‑specific delivery
crispro_top_description: Develop a computational connectivity biomarker to select patients with high tumor‑neuron synaptic activity for perampanel therapy

talk_title: Screening platform for disconnecting tumor‑tumor microtubule networks
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: I didn't want to focus on a target that actually kills the cells, but rather, a drug that actually disconnect the cells. | Speaker said: I don't see this drug as a really translational drug for now, but I wanted to really focus on the methods and designing a platform.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized that disconnecting tumor microtubules halts growth, yet admitted the PKC activator used is not clinically translatable and may have broad off‑target effects
mechanistic_blindspot: Broad PKC activation affects multiple pathways beyond tumor microtubule disconnection
crispro_top_description: Leverage CrisPRO's computational spine to predict selective PKC downstream effectors that disconnect microtubules without global PKC activation

talk_title: Myeloid monocyte and neutrophil dynamics in glioblastoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: We got a cellular resistant mechanism to ablating monocytes infiltration in the form of accelerated neutrophil infiltration. | Speaker said: Dexamethasone inhibits IL‑1, which is a major pro‑inflammatory cytokine, and changes completely myeloid and lymphoid profile in this tumor.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that neutrophil netosis drives immunosuppression, yet earlier emphasis on monocyte targeting suggests a contradictory focus on a single myeloid arm
mechanistic_blindspot: MPO‑mediated netosis creates immunosuppressive macrophages; inhibition leads to increased neutrophil numbers but altered phenotype
crispro_top_description: Re‑engineer CCR2 inhibitors with concurrent neutrophil/MPO modulation to prevent compensatory infiltration

institution: Fondazione Istituto Nazionale dei Tumori di Milano
talk_title: Final results of the INFINITY study investigating tremelimumab and durvalumab as definitive non-operative management for MSI-high gastric cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the optimal strategy for these patients in terms of treatment duration, treatment regimen, and treatment goal still needs to be established in larger randomized or adaptive trials." | Speaker said: "Importantly, after the results of cohort one and following review by an independent data monitoring committee, patients with a T4 disease were excluded from enrollment in cohort two due to their low probability of achieving a clinical complete response after only three months of immunotherapy."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high baseline myeloid infiltration and EMT programs in non‑responders, then concluded that non‑operative management is a viable option for "carefully selected patients" without specifying how to exclude the myeloid‑rich subgroup
failing_target: Tremelimumab plus durvalumab dual checkpoint regimen
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of efficacy in T4 disease and in patients with baseline myeloid-rich, EMT-associated tumor microenvironment
crispro_top_description: Baseline myeloid infiltration and EMT signatures predict lack of clinical complete response but were not used for patient selection

talk_title: Interim results from the phase 2 MATIS trial of IPH5201 (CD39 inhibitor) combined with durvalumab and platinum chemotherapy in resectable NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The primary endpoint of the study was the pathological complete response rate, safety, EFS, DFS, overall survival, feasibility of the surgery, major pathological response, overall response rate, PK, and anti‑drug antibodies were secondary endpoints." | Speaker said: "The trial has no power to make definitive comparison or for formal hypothesis testing."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented robust CD39‑positive Treg depletion across all dose levels, yet concluded that the regimen shows only modest efficacy (27.5% PCR) without explaining the disconnect
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited activity in PD‑L1 low tumors and lack of clear correlation between CD39 expression and response

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed real‑time imaging of ADC internalization and lysosomal processing, then concluded that "we can now decide which drug will work for my patient" despite acknowledging that many tumors lack lysosomal activity, which contradicts the observed mechanistic limitation.
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of lysosomal expression in patient tumor prevents linker cleavage and payload release
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model lysosomal deficiency and stromal barriers to predict ADC failure and suggest alternative therapeutic strategies.
companies_to_monitor: LG AI Research; Vanderbilt University Medical Center

talk_title: Clonal hematopoiesis, aging and lung cancer: mechanistic insights from mouse and human studies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the incidence of lung cancer decreased in the canakinumab arm only in patients with TET2 mutation" | Speaker said: "we are still looking at the long‑term effect of clonal hematopoiesis after treating with immunotherapy"
crispro_top_description: TET2‑mutated clonal hematopoiesis predicts longer overall survival after neoadjuvant chemo‑immunotherapy, but this biomarker is not yet used prospectively.

talk_title: Tumor‑infiltrating clonal hematopoiesis (TICH) in early‑stage NSCLC: prevalence, dynamics and immune remodeling
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "patients with TICH, but not patients with blood‑only CHIP, have worse disease‑free survival" | Speaker said: "CHIP clones are expanding over the course of the disease, specifically in patients who did not receive adjuvant treatment"
crispro_top_description: TICH status predicts worse disease‑free survival and could be used to select patients for intensified adjuvant therapy.

talk_title: TFF2 deficiency amplifies IL‑1β‑driven inflammation and promotes aging‑associated gastric tumor progression
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "TFF2 protein is also declined in both the stomach and in the serum" | Speaker said: "restoring TFF2 with TFF2‑MSA reduced PMN‑MDSC accumulation and partially restored circulating CD8 T cells"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that aging increases PMN‑MDSCs and IL‑1β, then concluded that restoring TFF2 “limits the cascade and attenuates tumor progression” – contradictory because the underlying IL‑1β source (MDSCs) remains elevated in aged mice
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects of the albumin fusion on liver metabolism not explored
crispro_top_description: Combine CrisPRO‑identified IL‑1β pathway inhibitors with TFF2‑MSA to synergistically suppress MDSC expansion in aged gastric cancer.

talk_title: The role of aging in immune‑mediated reactivation from metastatic dormancy (melanoma)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "in the middle‑age condition we obtain an increase of the liver metastasis" | Speaker said: "POSN1 overexpression increased PMN‑MDSC cells and decreased gamma‑delta T cells"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that aged microenvironments increase PMN‑MDSCs and suppress gamma‑delta T cells, yet concluded that “age microenvironments promote immunosuppressive role of gamma‑delta T cells” – contradictory because gamma‑delta T cells are being depleted rather than activated
mechanistic_blindspot: Downstream signaling of POSN1 to arginase‑1 production not dissected
crispro_top_description: Target POSN1‑driven MDSC expansion with arginase‑1 inhibitors alongside gamma‑delta T‑cell adoptive transfer.

talk_title: Age‑stratified therapeutic strategies targeting chemo‑resistant subclones in triple‑negative breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "only one clone (barcode 25) significantly increases after paclitaxel treatment in both young and aged mice" | Speaker said: "pan‑HDAC inhibitor panobinostat combined with anti‑PD‑L1 markedly improved survival in aged mice"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that aged mice have poor response to paclitaxel yet concluded that “HDAC inhibition could be a potential modality from which we can build age‑stratified treatment options” – contradictory because the underlying resistance mechanism (barcode 25) is not linked to HDAC activity in the data presented
mechanistic_blindspot: Age‑related pharmacokinetic changes and reduced tumor‑intrinsic response not addressed
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could provide a computational framework to predict resistant subclone emergence without needing physical barcoding.

talk_title: Age‑dependent neuroimmune changes in glioblastoma reveal senescent microglia as a targetable vulnerability
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "senescence is significantly increased in the peritumoral region of older mice" | Speaker said: "senolytics decreased microglia beta‑galactosidase cells in the older brain and improved survival when combined with radiation and immunotherapy"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that senolytics clear senescent microglia in the peritumoral brain but not in the tumor core, yet concluded that senolytics improve overall survival, implying tumor‑intrinsic benefit despite lack of intratumoral target
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential blood‑brain barrier penetration variability not addressed
crispro_top_description: Senescent microglia signature could stratify older GBM patients for senolytic‑combined radiotherapy.

institution: University of Dresden / University of Heidelberg
talk_title: Multimodal and Agentic AI in Biomarker Discovery
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I wish I were, but I'm not. Jakob could not make it today..." | Speaker said: "AI is no longer the future, it's happening today." | Speaker said: "We cannot just like previously in AI, have one system that produces one output. We have to look at the procedures that the tool calls..." | Speaker said: "Agents are of course susceptible to many pitfalls. There's of course hallucinations, many other potential problems."
crispro_top_description: Class C AI-driven biomarkers lack standardized validation frameworks and prospective clinical utility data, creating an opening for CrisPRO to provide rigorous multi‑cohort validation pipelines.

talk_title: AI‑Enabled End‑to‑End Drug Discovery at Recursion
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We went from target identification to IND‑enabling studies in just under 18 months." | Speaker said: "We only needed about 200 molecules synthesized for RBM39 and 136 for CDK7, demonstrating speed." | Speaker said: "We are not selecting patients based on CDK7 expression yet, but we are continuously evaluating it." | Speaker said: "We used Tempus data to establish a causal relationship between CDK7 expression and poor prognosis."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted rapid AI‑driven molecule optimization (136 compounds in 12 months) but also admitted that patient selection for CDK7 is still not biomarker‑driven, creating a mismatch between claimed precision and current trial design.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory activity of CDK12/13 not fully explored
crispro_top_description: REC‑617 lacks a prospective CDK7‑high patient selection biomarker; CrisPRO can develop and validate a multimodal CDK7 expression signature.

talk_title: AI Transformation of Pharmaceutical R&D
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AI is no longer the future, it's our reality." | Speaker said: "We moved from handcrafted annotations to self‑supervision on raw multimodal data." | Speaker said: "Regulators now focus on extensive validation rather than mandatory explainability." | Speaker said: "We reduced pathologist annotation time from 40,000 hours to four and a half, even to zero, using foundation models."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker described a highly accurate AI pathology biomarker that received breakthrough device designation, yet simultaneously warned that “black‑box” concerns remain and regulators still demand extensive validation.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential over‑reliance on model predictions without mechanistic explainability may hinder regulatory acceptance
crispro_top_description: AstraZeneca’s pathology AI models still require large annotation efforts; CrisPRO can provide zero‑annotation foundation models to further reduce human effort.

talk_title: CODA: 3D Mapping of Tumors at Single-Cell Resolution
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you take a patient and they went through the pink path versus the blue path, and then you've got differences in image reconstruction, differences in post-processing, differences in segmentation, you end up with 1,000 different potential measurements, 1,000 different feature extractions coming out of the same patient" | Speaker said: "We discovered that actually, blood vessel and nerve follow beautiful scale-free fractal geometry across species and across developmental stages" | Speaker said: "All seven fallopian tubes that we assessed ... contain STIC lesions"
crispro_top_description: CODA reveals that many precancerous lesions are missed by standard 2D pathology; a computational pipeline could stratify patients based on 3D lesion burden for early intervention.

talk_title: Sequencing to Treatment: The Role of AI in Precision Oncology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AI methods have now improved enough that there are real opportunities to incorporate into our workflows" | Speaker said: "Applying AI methods to infer which of the variants detected in the tumor are specific to the tumor versus inherited germline variants or clonal hematopoiesis" | Speaker said: "We have a limitation at our own center. We're only one specific geography, maybe not representative of all sort of national and ancestral and ethnic groups"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that deep learning variant callers (e.g., DeepVariant) achieve high accuracy, then concluded that current probabilistic callers are still sufficient for clinical use — ignoring the demonstrated superiority of AI models
mechanistic_blindspot: Inability to distinguish somatic mutations from germline or clonal hematopoiesis without matched normal
crispro_top_description: AI can infer functional impact of missense variants using AlphaMisSense/AlphaGenome, addressing the blindspot of unknown resistance mechanisms in tumor‑only sequencing.
companies_to_monitor: AstraZeneca; Google DeepMind; Microsoft (for Azure AI services); IBM Watson Health

talk_title: Embracing Cancer Complexity: Practical Considerations for Translating AI to the Clinic
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you create a very complex model that's expecting all these different data streams to come in, in completeness every single time, it's going to be much harder for you to successfully deploy that model later on" | Speaker said: "We call meta‑analyses and collections of meta‑analyses the Farmer's Almanac... it's not going to provide us with the type of information that we may want for an individual patient" | Speaker said: "The more work that you need to do to get this real‑world data coming from different hospitals into a state where you can actually train and validate and test this model, that gap is going to be the same gap you're going to have when you're trying to deploy the model"
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can embed metadata‑quality scores into AI model training pipelines to reduce deployment failure due to heterogeneous real‑world data.

talk_title: Agentic AI for Accelerating Biomedical Discovery
Foundation Models and Virtual Cells for Single‑Cell Biology

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the models stay performant, and we understand performance as we implement and deploy them" | Speaker noted: "alerts were happening after treatment had already initiated"
crispro_top_description: Integrate CrisPRO’s biomarker stratification to enhance sepsis risk models for oncology patients

talk_title: Foundation Models for Computational Pathology and Virtual Patient Representations

talk_title: Interim results of a Phase 2 trial of neoadjuvant trastuzumab tucatinib combined with chemotherapy in locally advanced rectal carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "No patient progressed on this treatment during this introductory period." | Speaker noted: "Three patients who had a complete response recurred locally, but all these patients... have been salvaged without surgery." | Speaker admitted: "We don't know the KRAS status of all the screened patients, but it's an interesting question."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high CD8 infiltration in responders (implied by high response rates) yet concluded that HER2 blockade alone is sufficient without addressing the need for concurrent chemotherapy
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential intra‑tumoral HER2 heterogeneity not addressed; patients with lower HER2 IHC (2+) had poor responses
crispro_top_description: HER2 copy number appears more predictive than IHC; CrisPRO could develop a computational model to integrate copy number and ctDNA dynamics for patient selection.

talk_title: Phase 2 neoadjuvant study of zenodatumab in early‑stage HER2‑positive breast cancer (NEO3NHER)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "All patients in this study that had PCR were HER2 3+, had ERBB2 amplification on whole exome sequencing, were HER2 subtype high on PAM50." | Speaker noted: "There were no Grade 3 or higher treatment‑related adverse events." | Speaker admitted: "No clinical variables were associated with PCR, albeit we had a limited sample size."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented robust HER2‑driven molecular responses (ERBB2 amplification, HER2‑high PAM50) yet concluded that chemotherapy can be omitted despite modest PCR rate (30%)
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of activity in HER2 IHC 2+ tumors; response appears limited to HER2 3+ and ERBB2‑amplified cases
crispro_top_description: Identify HER2‑high transcriptional signatures (e.g., HER2‑RNA, PAM50) that predict PCR to enrich future trials.

talk_title: Whole exome sequencing tumor‑informed ctDNA detection after neoadjuvant treatment predicts non‑pCR and distant recurrence in early triple‑negative breast cancer (NSABP B‑59/GEPRDUOS)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Patients who were ctDNA positive after neoadjuvant treatment had a 54% recurrence rate versus 6% for ctDNA negative patients." | Speaker noted: "CtDNA clearance showed 97.9% specificity for non‑pCR and 85.7% positive predictive value for not achieving pathological complete response." | Speaker admitted: "No statistical evaluation could be performed due to small numbers for some sub‑analyses."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ctDNA negativity strongly predicts long‑term outcomes yet concluded that the current standard of care remains unchanged without ctDNA‑guided therapy
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of integration of ctDNA dynamics into real‑time treatment adaptation; patients with persistent ctDNA were not escalated
crispro_top_description: Incorporate real‑time ctDNA clearance as a trigger for treatment escalation in future neoadjuvant trials for triple‑negative breast cancer.

talk_title: Perioperative atezolizumab plus chemotherapy in resectable stage II/III NSCLC – phase III interim analysis and MRD analysis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The hazard ratio for EFS was 0.45, with a two‑year EFS rate of 74.8% versus 55.5% for placebo." | Speaker noted: "Grade 3‑4 immune‑related adverse events were 4.4% in the atezolizumab arm versus 1.6% in placebo." | Speaker admitted: "The trial excluded patients with EGFR mutations and ALK translocations, limiting applicability to those molecular subgroups."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented strong EFS benefit yet acknowledged that patients with driver mutations (EGFR/ALK) were excluded, contradicting the claim of a broadly applicable peri‑operative regimen
mechanistic_blindspot: Exclusion of EGFR/ALK‑mutant patients prevents assessment of efficacy in those high‑risk molecular subgroups
crispro_top_description: Develop a predictive model integrating PD‑L1 TPS, ctDNA clearance, and tumor mutational burden to refine patient selection.

talk_title: Clinical outcomes by genomic markers and ctDNA dynamics with perioperative nivolumab for resectable NSCLC (CheckMate 770)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Perioperative nivo demonstrated statistically significant and clinically meaningful EFS benefit versus placebo." | Speaker noted: "Patients with KEAP1, SDK11, CDKN2A, or SMARCA4 alterations had worse outcomes on placebo but similar outcomes on nivo." | Speaker admitted: "Sample sizes for mutation subgroups were small, and data need to be interpreted with caution."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted that ctDNA clearance is a strong predictor of EFS yet concluded that genomic alterations have limited predictive value, despite showing worse outcomes on placebo for those alterations.
failing_target: Nivolumab in patients with co‑alterations (KEAP1, SDK11, CDKN2A, SMARCA4)
mechanistic_blindspot: Unclear impact of these co‑alterations on immune evasion; limited data to guide therapy
crispro_top_description: Create a CrisPRO‑driven composite score combining ctDNA clearance, nodal status, and key genomic alterations to better select patients for peri‑operative nivolumab.

talk_title: Feasibility and efficacy of perioperative nivolumab with or without relatlimab for potentially resectable HCC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "No patients experienced any treatment‑related adverse event that precluded surgery." | Speaker noted: "Three of four patients who achieved a complete radiographic response later recurred, suggesting radiographic CR may not be fully ablative." | Speaker admitted: "LAG‑3 receptor occupancy was markedly lower in tumor‑infiltrating lymphocytes compared to peripheral blood, indicating limited tumor penetration at the current dose."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented robust safety and feasibility yet concluded that the regimen may still be insufficient for durable tumor eradication, as evidenced by recurrences after radiographic CR.
mechanistic_blindspot: Low LAG‑3 occupancy in tumor suggests insufficient drug delivery to the hepatic tumor microenvironment
crispro_top_description: Explore addition of anti‑VEGF or vascular normalizing agents to improve tumor penetration of relatlimab.

talk_title: Origins of founder gene dosage mutations in normal breast epithelium and age‑related changes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the vast majority of cells here, much less than 1% of the cells seem to have copy number gains" | Speaker said: "we don't know why we see fewer events in organoids – it's frustrating because we'd like to study this functionally" | Speaker said: "we think that this may represent lesions that were microscopically invisible as precursor lesions but were already, say, partially on their way to forming more established cancers"
crispro_top_description: Develop a CrisPRO‑based assay to quantify low‑frequency 1q gain and 10q loss in normal breast tissue as a risk predictor for future breast cancer.

talk_title: Single‑cell DNA profiling of normal breast epithelium reveals age‑linked aneuploidy and lineage‑specific copy‑number events
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "about 5% of the epithelial cells were aneuploid" | Speaker said: "age was the only factor that we found that seemed to really correlate with increases of these cells" | Speaker said: "we didn't see a lot of copy‑neutral LOH"
crispro_top_description: Create a CrisPRO pipeline to integrate single‑cell DNA and ATAC data for early detection of lineage‑specific aneuploid clones in normal breast tissue.

talk_title: Aneuploidy patterns as predictors of immune evasion and therapy response across cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "9p loss is emerging as a strong predictor of immunoevasion across several tumor types" | Speaker said: "1q gain is associated with immune evasion and poor immunotherapy response in endometrial cancer" | Speaker said: "kidney cancer often behaves in the opposite way and has 1q gain associated with immune hot microenvironment"
mechanistic_blindspot: Specific driver genes on 9p (e.g., antigen processing genes) not yet identified
crispro_top_description: Develop CrisPRO models to pinpoint the driver genes within 9p loss that mediate immune evasion, enabling a refined companion diagnostic.

talk_title: Regulatory perspective on rare cancer drug development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "It's not that there's a lesser bar for rare cancers compared to less rare cancers." | Speaker said: "We do recommend careful consideration of whether randomized designs are the most appropriate design in rare cancers." | Speaker said: "Single‑arm designs are not always the ideal approach."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that accelerated approval can be based on intermediate endpoints such as ORR, then concluded that "substantial evidence of effectiveness" is required for all approvals, creating tension between a lower evidentiary bar and the stated need for robust data
mechanistic_blindspot: lack of a control arm makes it impossible to separate drug effect from natural disease course
crispro_top_description: Many rare‑cancer single‑arm trials lack biomarker‑driven patient selection, which could be addressed by CrisPRO's computational biomarker platform.

talk_title: Barriers to clinical development of therapeutics for chronic myelomonocytic leukemia
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "...there are no FDA‑approved drugs that alter the natural history of the disease." | Speaker said: "...the moral of this story is that really azacitidine, decitabine, and oral decitabine in Covi were all approved really in a clinical trial that included MDS patients." | Speaker said: "...the short answer in CMML is we're trying...the biggest challenge here is that it is among the most clinically heterogeneous diseases."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted convergent biology (GM‑CSF hypersensitivity) as a therapeutic target, yet noted that “there are no FDA‑approved drugs that alter the natural history of the disease,” indicating a gap between mechanistic insight and clinical success
mechanistic_blindspot: Trials enrolled CMML patients within broader MDS studies, diluting disease‑specific signal
crispro_top_description: Targeting GM‑CSF signaling (e.g., JAK2 inhibition, anti‑GM‑CSF antibodies) could overcome the convergent pathway identified by the speaker.

talk_title: Development of the first FDA‑approved treatment for women with low‑grade serous ovarian cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "...single‑agent MEK inhibitors showed initial promising response but were limited by toxicity." | Speaker said: "...the combination therapy had a higher response rate and similar side effects to the single agent avutametinib." | Speaker said: "...we chose not to go straight into a large Phase 3 study looking at the frontline setting."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented strong preclinical rationale that intermittent dosing would reduce toxicity, yet the trial still reported similar side‑effect profiles between combination and higher‑dose single‑agent arms, indicating a mismatch between dosing hypothesis and clinical outcome
mechanistic_blindspot: Continuous dosing schedule caused dose‑limiting adverse events, limiting exposure
crispro_top_description: Develop additional predictive biomarkers (e.g., MAPK pathway activation signatures) to identify KRAS‑wildtype responders.

talk_title: Revolutionizing Phase I Trials Using Multidimensional Toxicity, Efficacy, and PRO Integration
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't probably often spend enough time understanding the efficacy readout in early phase trials." | Speaker said: "We have a package, it's called a phase one RMD. It's an R package for repeated measures dose-finding designs." | Speaker said: "We didn't incorporate the PROs in the first two phases, it was really in the final dose recommendation." | Speaker said: "We have a design in press, but we really don't have a trial with this design yet."
crispro_top_description: Speaker highlighted lack of molecular biomarker integration in early phase dose optimization, suggesting an opportunity for CrisPRO to provide biomarker-driven stratification.

talk_title: Revolutionize in Phase I Trials Using the BOIN Framework and Backfill
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "FDA has determined that the statistical methodology of the local BOIN design under the naive informative prior is suitable for fit for purpose designation in 2021." | Speaker said: "We observed that the risk of underdosing is the lowest in general for BOIN compared to 3+3." | Speaker said: "Agencies are okay to override the rule because the long-term transition using one out of three makes statistical sense." | Speaker said: "The back-fill BOIN design provides a principle to rigorous approach that can address those issues and safeguard patient safety."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that BOIN reduces underdosing risk compared to 3+3, then concluded that agencies are comfortable with overriding the rule for one out of three patients, which contradicts the emphasis on strict safety boundaries.
mechanistic_blindspot: Using a high target DLT rate (e.g., 0.3) can lead to overly permissive escalation boundaries, increasing risk of exposing patients to unsafe doses.
crispro_top_description: Incorporate biomarker-driven strata into BOIN designs to prevent signal dilution identified in trial dilution risk.

talk_title: Hypercholesterolemia, 27‑hydroxycholesterol and breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have proven that 27‑hydroxycholesterol was related with respect to levels of cholesterol" | Speaker said: "we showed that those patients on a statin could lower LDL cholesterol and also lower 27‑hydroxycholesterol"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that 27HC is a weak partial estrogen agonist yet concluded that statins or vitamin D supplementation can fully mitigate its pro‑tumorigenic effects without addressing the resistant subclones
mechanistic_blindspot: Failure to address emergence of 27HC‑resistant subclones that become highly metastatic
crispro_top_description: Target the GPX4‑dependent ferroptosis resistance pathway in 27HC‑selected metastatic clones

talk_title: Bidirectional cardio‑oncology: shared risk factors and causal links
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have found that non‑atherosclerotic forms of disease, like valve disease or arrhythmia, do not appear to put you at higher risk of cancer" | Speaker said: "addressing all of the known cardiovascular risk factors may have benefit"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented epidemiologic data showing strong cancer risk from atherosclerosis yet concluded that simple risk‑factor control (e.g., statins) will halve cancer incidence without presenting interventional trial evidence
failing_target: Atherosclerotic disease as a risk factor for cancer
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of mechanistic data linking specific plaque components to tumor initiation
crispro_top_description: Create a composite risk score integrating atherosclerotic burden (e.g., CAC score) with cancer incidence to identify high‑risk survivors

talk_title: Cancer‑driven atherosclerosis via LRG1 and TNF
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "tumor‑bearing mice had plaques riddled with fragile neovessels" | Speaker said: "blocking TNF with etanercept reduced plaque size and angiogenesis"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TNF drives plaque neovascularization yet advocated TNF blockade despite its known risk of worsening heart failure

mechanistic_blindspot: TNF blockers have a black‑box warning for fluid retention in heart failure patients
crispro_top_description: Pair TNF inhibition with a GPX4‑targeting agent to mitigate fluid‑retention while preserving anti‑metastatic effect

talk_title: Ischemic cardiovascular events reprogram bone marrow to promote tumor growth
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "myocardial infarction doubles tumor volume and increases metastatic foci" | Speaker said: "depleting Ly6C high monocytes abolishes the MI‑induced tumor growth advantage"

session_title: Beyond Chromosomes, Mechanisms, Consequences, and Therapeutic Opportunities of Extrachromosomal DNA

session_title: Beyond Chromosomes, Mechanisms, Consequences, and Therapeutic Opportunities of Extrachromosomal DNA

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we think this is a therapeutic vulnerability that we should exploit | Speaker said: we don't have direct experimental evidence for cGAS-STING activation yet

session_title: Beyond Chromosomes, Mechanisms, Consequences, and Therapeutic Opportunities of Extrachromosomal DNA
talk_title: Consequences of ecDNA loss: micronucleation, cell fitness, and therapeutic targeting of retention
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we think that ecDNA retention is a therapeutically actionable liability | Speaker admitted: we haven't investigated DNA repair in micronuclei yet

crispro_top_description: Incorporate a retention‑element activity biomarker to stratify patients in future ecDNA‑targeted trials

session_title: Beyond Genetics: Macroenvironmental and Microenvironmental Determinants of Cancer
talk_title: DNA Repair, Radon Exposure, and Lung Cancer Disparities
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have shown that African American non‑small cell lung cancer patients have a signature, HRD signature, and we think that this could be a novel genomic risk factor." | Speaker said: "Radon actually causes 3% to 4% of all lung cancer cases. In never smokers, it's the leading environmental cause of the disease." | Speaker said: "We hypothesize that genetic ancestry was associated with population‑specific germline pathogenic variants in this pathway in lung cancer." | Speaker said: "We are testing the hypothesis that free or low‑cost community‑based testing would reduce radon testing disparities in high radon areas."

institution: Boston University School of Public Health
session_title: Beyond Genetics: Macroenvironmental and Microenvironmental Determinants of Cancer
talk_title: Environmental Risk Factors for Prostate Cancer Pathology in Men of African Ancestry
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We see a much greater tumor mutational burden among men of African ancestry." | Speaker said: "There were 63 driver mutations specific to African ancestry populations in epigenetic machinery genes." | Speaker said: "Endocrine‑disrupting chemicals can alter hormone activity and may contribute to more aggressive prostate cancers." | Speaker said: "Timing of exposure may be critical… prenatal, perinatal, or puberty windows may increase susceptibility."

institution: Gladstone Institute for Cancer Immunotherapy / University of California, San Francisco
session_title: Beyond Genetics: Macroenvironmental and Microenvironmental Determinants of Cancer
talk_title: Spatially Organized Immune Hubs in Human Tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "These CXCL3 ligand hubs can distinguish patients that are poised to respond to immunotherapy and lung cancer." | Speaker said: "In oral dysplasia, these hubs distinguish patients that are poised to progress to cancer." | Speaker said: "We see that these hubs are enriched at the stroma‑epithelium interface and that CD8+ CXCL3‑positive T cells infiltrate the cancer epithelium." | Speaker said: "Translating that into what it actually means for patient outcome can be quite difficult."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that CXCL3 ligand hubs predict response to checkpoint blockade, then concluded that "translating that into what it actually means for patient outcome can be quite difficult" – contradiction between strong predictive spatial signal and uncertainty about clinical utility.
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can provide a computational pipeline to quantify CXCL3 ligand hub density from standard histology images, offering a lower‑cost alternative to MERFISH/Xenium.

talk_title: Hematopoietic loss of Y and its implications in disease
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we basically found this by looking in a cohort of aging men called ULSAM... men with loss of Y in more than one-third of their blood cells had survival only half as long as other men" | Speaker said: "we identified that men with loss of Y in more than 5% of their blood cells... that's a high number" | Speaker said: "we don't know if it's loss of Y in tumors or immune cells... we will be able to show that, I hope" | Speaker said: "population screening is not the way to go... we need much more information"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed extensive preclinical data that LOY drives fibrosis and immune dysfunction, then concluded that population screening for LOY is not feasible, contradicting the implication that LOY is a strong actionable biomarker
failing_target: Loss of Y chromosome in hematopoietic cells
mechanistic_blindspot: Direct functional consequences of LOY on immune cell subsets (e.g., T‑cell exhaustion, macrophage polarization) are not fully addressed in current therapeutic strategies
crispro_top_description: Develop a computational pipeline to quantify LOY in specific immune cell subsets (e.g., CD8+ T cells, monocytes) from bulk or single‑cell sequencing data to enable patient stratification for anti‑fibrotic or checkpoint therapies.

talk_title: From discovery to FDA‑approved HIF‑2α inhibitor belzutifan
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "30% of the patients have a primary progressive disease" | Speaker said: "We found that the tumors that were sensitive had significantly higher levels of HIF‑2α than the tumors that were resistant" | Speaker said: "We are not going to go through all the data because Eric is going to go into a lot of detail"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that HIF‑2α high tumors regress with belzutifan, then concluded that "30% of patients have primary progressive disease" indicating a disconnect between the biomarker observation and overall efficacy claim
mechanistic_blindspot: Tumors with low HIF‑2α expression do not respond, suggesting reliance on alternative pathways such as HIF‑1α or non‑HIF mechanisms
crispro_top_description: Develop a predictive assay for tumor HIF‑2α protein/mRNA levels to enrich for responders and avoid primary resistance.

talk_title: Clinical data of HIF inhibition in VHL syndrome
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't have a therapy that completely eradicates these lesions" | Speaker said: "We don't know why it's not working in a subset of individuals" | Speaker said: "We need more data and an explanation for upfront and acquired resistance"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high ORR (70%) and reduction in surgical procedures, then admitted "we don't have a therapy that completely eradicates these lesions" indicating a mismatch between efficacy claim and curative potential
mechanistic_blindspot: Lesions that are HIF‑2α independent (possible HIF‑1α driven or alternative pathways)
crispro_top_description: Create a composite VHL lesion biomarker (HIF‑2α IHC + VHL mutation type) to predict which patients benefit most.

talk_title: HIF inhibition across sporadic renal cell carcinoma settings
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Primary PD rate for monotherapy is quite high, a third of patients will have primary progression" | Speaker said: "We don't yet know the mechanism there" | Speaker said: "There is a tail‑of‑the‑curve effect of belzutifan across multiple indications"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed a modest ORR (~23%) and high primary PD rate, then highlighted a “tail‑of‑the‑curve” durability claim, creating tension between low initial efficacy and long‑term benefit
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of HIF‑2α dependency in many advanced tumors; possible reliance on HIF‑1α or alternative oncogenic pathways
crispro_top_description: Validate serum IPAG reduction as a predictive biomarker to select patients likely to achieve durable responses.

talk_title: Systemic reprogramming in cancer cachexia using Drosophila and mouse models
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we believe that the paraneoplastic effect of the tumor goes beyond muscle and fat wasting" | Speaker said: "we saw that most of the biomass of the growing tumor actually derived from the degrading organs and not food" | Speaker said: "we identified one that behaved exactly like this, which is called subermesa... reduces malignant tumor growth but does not affect benign growth or normal development"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that tumor autophagy provides nutrients to support growth, then concluded that targeting autophagy alone will block tumor growth – ignoring that nutrient transporters (SLC7A9) can bypass autophagy-derived supply
mechanistic_blindspot: Redundancy with SLC7A9; targeting SLC7A11 alone may be insufficient due to compensatory SLC7A9 activity
crispro_top_description: Targeting SLC7A9 alongside SLC7A11 could overcome redundancy and prevent tumor utilization of host-derived cysteine

talk_title: VHL loss–driven HIF‑2α–PTHrP axis mediates cachexia in clear‑cell renal carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the phenotype was independent from the tumor size" | Speaker said: "treatment with HIF‑2α inhibitor reduced PTHrP levels and improved body weight" | Speaker said: "PTHLH knockout mice maintained body weight and survived"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PTHrP drives both hypercalcemia and weight loss, then concluded that HIF‑2α inhibition improves weight independent of tumor burden – ignoring that tumor burden itself can also influence cachexia
mechanistic_blindspot: Cachexia improvement observed but overall survival benefit not demonstrated in current trials
crispro_top_description: Baseline plasma PTHrP could identify RCC patients who would benefit most from HIF‑2α inhibition for cachexia mitigation
cited_competitors: Belzutifan (Bayer); NKT2152 (Niken Therapeutics); Immune checkpoint inhibitors; VEGF‑TK inhibitors
companies_to_monitor: Bayer; Niken Therapeutics; Companies developing PTH1R antagonists

talk_title: Whole genome duplication as a predictor of metastasis for pan-cancer predictor
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we hope today's talk, I'm able to persuade you that whole genome doubling emerges as a promising biomarker | Speaker said: I think the final key question we want to ask is, is everything we see independent of TP53?
crispro_top_description: Whole genome duplication could be integrated into a computational risk model to stratify early-stage patients for intensified surveillance.

talk_title: Tissue-based homologous recombination deficiency status prediction in breast, ovarian, and pancreatic cancers

crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could re‑analyze existing Guardant360 sequencing data to refine HRD scoring and improve specificity.

talk_title: Integrated multi-omic approaches in resectable hepatocellular carcinoma with microvascular invasion
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: TOP2A consistently emerged as a therapeutic target | Speaker said: patients in the score high group demonstrate poorer outcomes with immune checkpoint therapy
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TOP2A inhibition is effective in vitro yet concluded that current standard therapies (atezolizumab) are less effective in MVI‑high patients
failing_target: Immune checkpoint inhibitors in MVI‑high HCC
mechanistic_blindspot: Reduced immune infiltration and chemokine signaling in MVI‑high tumors
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could identify synergistic partners for TOP2A inhibition based on the cell‑cycle and DNA repair signatures in MVI‑high HCC.

talk_title: Stromal immune composition significantly contributes to adenoma polyp recurrence or progression to cancer

crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can integrate the spatial transcriptomic signatures (IgA plasma cell loss, TUFT cell increase) into a predictive risk score for polyp recurrence.

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: whole exome sequencing based HRD scoring provided independent prognostic information for PFS | Speaker said: prospective validation in independent cohort is warranted and is currently ongoing

mechanistic_blindspot: Cutoff may not be optimal across all histologies and tumor purity levels
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can simulate alternative HRD cutoffs and enrollment criteria to optimize future prospective trials.

talk_title: Utility of CA19-9 for the prediction of asymptomatic pancreatic cancer among patients with new onset diabetes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: CA19-9 had an overall sensitivity of 83% with greater than 97% specificity in the NOD cohort | Speaker said: the PPV was around 70% in the NOD population
mechanistic_blindspot: Elevated CA19-9 in benign GI conditions and other cancers reduces specificity in real‑world settings
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can integrate longitudinal CA19-9 trajectories with additional markers (ALGY1, TIMP1) to improve early detection algorithms.

talk_title: Precision diagnostics for early melanoma detection using spatial biology and AI‑guided image analysis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: BRAF V600 mutation was highly expressed in benign nevus compared to thin melanoma | Speaker said: module scores allowed lesion classification into either benign or malignant
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker reported BRAF V600E enrichment in benign nevi yet highlighted BRAF as a driver mutation in melanoma, creating a paradox between mutation prevalence and malignancy
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can generate predictive gene‑expression maps from routine H&E slides, bypassing costly spatial transcriptomics.

talk_title: Homeostatic collapse in pancreatic cancer cachexia via ZIP4
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the only effective treatment for pancreatic cancer is a surgical procedure called Whipple procedure. However, no matter how aggressive the surgeons are, only 10%, 15% of the patients are eligible for surgery." | Speaker said: "we're worried about the non-specific side effects" when asked about regulating CREB.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects of ZIP4 inhibition on normal zinc homeostasis not addressed
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could model zinc‑dependent transcriptional networks downstream of ZIP4 to identify alternative nodes that bypass ZIP4 inhibition.

talk_title: Liver molecular clock disruption and cachexia
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we found a severe disruption of the molecular clock of the liver, and in particular, completely blunted REVERB expression" | Speaker said: "when we reconstituted REVERB expression in the liver, this had an impact on weight loss"

crispro_top_description: Identify patients with low hepatic REVERB expression using transcriptomic signatures to select for REVERB‑targeted therapies.

talk_title: ADAMTSL4 as a cachexia driver via TGF‑β activation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "ADAMTSL4 levels were increased in cachexia‑inducing tumor models and correlated with weight loss" | Speaker said: "knocking down ADAMTSL4 in the tumor ameliorated weight loss and muscle atrophy"
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory activation of other ADAMTS‑like proteins not explored
crispro_top_description: Develop CrisPRO‑guided small molecules that disrupt ADAMTSL4‑LAP interaction as an alternative to antibodies.

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we applied Ed Resnick's sliding BMI trajectory code to 6,000 patients and identified pre‑cachexia, cachexia, and recovery episodes" | Speaker said: "clusters based on CT body composition revealed a splenomegaly phenotype with high IL‑6, GDF15 and poor survival"
