talk_title: Mathematical modeling of inflammation‑weight dynamics in cachexia
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the model predicts a saddle‑node bifurcation that represents the point of no return for terminal cachexia" | Speaker said: "temporary food restriction experiments validated the model’s prediction of identical weight loss but divergent recovery slopes"
crispro_top_description: Use CrisPRO to calibrate the mathematical model with patient‑level multi‑omics, enabling personalized prediction of cachexia tipping points.

talk_title: Precision Cancer Prevention: Immunoprevention and p53 Reactivation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have identified an agent which we call REM-491" | Speaker said: "we have not achieved the maximum tolerated dose yet" | Speaker said: "we saw no evidence of adverse effects"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed robust CD8 T‑cell responses and tumor reduction in an aggressive mouse model, then concluded that the agent is ready for early‑phase clinical trials despite lacking target‑engagement data
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential lack of target engagement confirmation in vivo
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model mutant p53 structural pockets to predict which mutations are most likely to be reactivated by small molecules, guiding optimization of REM-491 analogs

talk_title: Metabolic vulnerabilities of Nrf2‑active cancer cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we can't fully distinguish whether the labeled cystine entered as cystine or cysteine" | Speaker said: "we were surprised to find that knocking out both disulfide reductases only had a moderate effect on tumor number" | Speaker said: "we think that the microenvironment is very different in pancreas compared to lung"
crispro_top_description: Lung tumors show high interstitial cysteine but low cystine, suggesting cystine‑uptake inhibitors would be effective only in cystine‑dependent tumors.

talk_title: Mitochondrial protein degradation drives T‑cell exhaustion via regulatory heme
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "they start to degrade mitochondria protein to ensure they can still stay there" | Speaker said: "once you treat the cells with MG132, those T cell accumulate damaged mitochondria, they are terminal exhausted T cell. They express very low level of Bach2. But once you treat the MG132, you start to see Bach2 increase" | Speaker said: "we think that this degradation machinery can trigger the exhaustion commitment"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that damaged mitochondria lead to increased proteasome‑mediated degradation of mitochondrial proteins, yet concluded that blocking proteasome activity (with bortezomib) restores stem‑like T‑cell phenotype, ignoring that proteasome inhibition could also impair antigen processing
failing_target: Proteasome activity in exhausted T cells
mechanistic_blindspot: Degradation of mitochondrial hemoproteins releases free heme that binds BACH2, driving exhaustion
crispro_top_description: Targeting the heme‑BACH2 interaction could prevent exhaustion without global proteasome inhibition.

talk_title: Mitochondrial chaperone TREM1 regulates immunosuppressive tumor‑associated macrophages
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "when you delete TREM1, mitochondrial function become hyper" | Speaker said: "TIM‑4 activation suppresses TREM1 expression via AMPK‑MBK signaling" | Speaker said: "combination of TIM‑4 antibody and GMG3 inhibitor strongly reduces tumor burden"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TREM1 loss increases mitochondrial activity yet concluded that restoring TREM1 will suppress oxidative phosphorylation, without addressing how TREM1 mechanistically restrains complex II activity
mechanistic_blindspot: Loss of TREM1 leads to hyperactive mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation that fuels immunosuppressive M2‑like phenotype
crispro_top_description: Develop a diagnostic assay for TIM‑4 high vs low TAMs to select patients for TIM‑4 antibody therapy.

talk_title: Cancer and Telomere Disorders
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the risk of cancer was about three-fold higher than in the general population" | Speaker said: "the most common solid tumor in TBDs is head and neck squamous cell carcinoma... risk was about 50-fold higher" | Speaker said: "we don't really know what the short answer is... we don't really know how to treat these cancers"
mechanistic_blindspot: patients with telomere biology disorders have bone marrow failure that makes them intolerant to DNA-damaging agents
crispro_top_description: Lack of prospective monitoring of clonal hematopoiesis mutations to predict cancer risk in TBD patients.

talk_title: Lynch Syndrome: Identification, Risk Modeling, and Screening Strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Lynch syndrome is the most common inherited GI cancer syndrome... prevalence is 1 in 350" | Speaker said: "the uptake of cascade testing at best is only 40%" | Speaker said: "we see a striking difference, particularly in PMS2, where cancer risks deviate only after age 60"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high prevalence of MSI‑high tumors in Lynch carriers but concluded that universal MSI‑high screening alone will miss carriers with MSI‑intermediate or stable tumors
mechanistic_blindspot: one-size-fits-all colonoscopy intervals ignore genotype-specific cancer risk trajectories
crispro_top_description: Need for genotype‑specific risk scores to guide colonoscopy frequency.

talk_title: Mathematical modeling drives adaptive therapy clinical trials
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Resistance should have a cost when compared to the transformed tumor cells that are not resistant" | Speaker said: "However, the 1205LU population appears to show no difference in proliferative ability"

session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
talk_title: Personalized Neoantigen Vaccines in Glioblastoma
rhetorical_signals: I'm a little bit nervous because I haven't presented this before | This is still a work in progress | maybe we need actually better professional antigen-presenting cells to be present at the time of priming to really launch that CD8 positive response

session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
talk_title: mRNA Vaccines as Immune Adjuvants to Checkpoint Blockade
rhetorical_signals: we like to propose this very linear story... But in reality, I think that the reality here is a little more complex | this retrospective data we really have to take with a grain of salt | designed not to produce a strong interferon alpha burst
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker notes that COVID vaccines were specifically designed to avoid TLR7 activation (via N1-methylpseudouridine), yet they still trigger a massive interferon alpha surge via the MDA5 'backdoor' pathway.

crispro_top_description: Developing an off-the-shelf mRNA adjuvant that optimizes the MDA5/IFN-alpha pathway without the need for personalization.

talk_title: First‑in‑human KIR‑CAR targeting mesothelin in solid tumors

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed pre‑clinical mouse data where Smoothened inhibition + gemcitabine improved survival, then concluded that the same approach in humans made tumors progress faster — ignoring the contradictory outcome | Speaker presented IL‑1β blockade pre‑clinical promise in lung cancer, yet reported no benefit in pancreatic cancer patients, yet still suggested IL‑1β as a viable target without reconciling the failure

talk_title: CD105-defined fibroblast lineages and tissue‑specific fibroblast responses
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: CD105 positive fibroblasts remain CD105 positive in culture, indicating a stable lineage | Speaker said: CD105 negative fibroblasts suppress tumor growth in a CD45‑dependent manner | Speaker said: fibroblast responses to tumor signals cluster by tissue of origin rather than tumor type
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that CD105 positive fibroblasts accelerate tumor growth, yet CD105 is a TGF‑beta co‑receptor that is up‑regulated by TGF‑beta, suggesting that blocking TGF‑beta (as done in other trials) might paradoxically reduce a tumor‑promoting fibroblast subset

talk_title: Generative models for disease dynamics and scientific reasoning
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Flow matching is behind a lot of the state-of-the-art visual generation models today." | Speaker said: "We still see performance gets worse the more out of distribution you are, but I think it's still overall promising trajectories here." | Speaker said: "The actual flow matching is done in the latent space."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that the model can generate smooth continuous trajectories (observation) but then admitted that modeling discrete events like apoptosis remains a challenge (contradiction).
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited ability to model discrete biological events such as apoptosis within a continuous latent trajectory
crispro_top_description: Speaker identified difficulty modeling discrete events (e.g., apoptosis) with continuous flow models.

talk_title: Reliable medical AI: agents that plan, evaluate, and verify
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Many systems drop drastically in performance if they have to actually do the workup on their own." | Speaker said: "We need to develop new methods, new strategies to ground and verify outputs of AI systems." | Speaker said: "We propose a process reward model that can check each reasoning step against literature and guidelines."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented a vision of fully reliable agents (observation) but simultaneously admitted that current models still hallucinate and need step‑wise verification (contradiction).
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of grounding in structured medical knowledge leading to hallucinations
crispro_top_description: Need for structured, step‑wise verification suggests designing trials that capture intermediate reasoning checkpoints.

talk_title: Distinguishing selection in normal tissues from cancer causation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Are those necessarily mutations that cause cancer, or are maybe some of them just an inheritance from the normal tissue evolution that precedes carcinogenesis as we age?" | Speaker said: "I think an open question is how large the overlap is and in which of these categories any one individual mutation belongs" | Speaker said: "We don't have normal tissue data from the same people that we analyze the cancers from" | Speaker said: "If you don't have that data, then age data is super helpful because genes that are frequency mutated in younger cancer patients are very likely to be causal"

mechanistic_blindspot: PIK3CA appears equally frequent in cancer and normal tissue, suggesting limited carcinogenic effect
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could model the differential selection pressure of mutations like PIK3CA across normal and tumor tissues to predict true drivers

talk_title: Clonal evolution, promotion, and the role of inflammation in cancer

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Mouse tumors have less mutations, less copy number alterations, giving a cleaner view on dependencies." | Speaker said: "We found this dependency of RAS on CDKN2A loss, and if you do the same kind of analysis in humans, you don't see that pattern until you remove P53-inactivated tumors."

talk_title: Serial Plasma Comprehensive Genomic Profiling Detects Therapy Resistance and Guides Management of Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "patients who received a genotype-targeted therapy based on an actionable alteration identified by PCGP had significantly better overall survival compared to those who did not." | Speaker said: "we were able to capture these structural alterations by liquid biopsy, but there were some instances of discordance."

institution: University of Pittsburgh Hillman Cancer Center
talk_title: Feasibility of Plasma ctDNA for Detecting Brain Metastasis Specific Alterations in Lung Cancer

crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can integrate ctDNA VAF dynamics with radiotherapy timing to define optimal sampling windows for brain metastasis detection.

talk_title: AXL mRNA Overexpression in CTCs as a New Potential Liquid Biopsy Biomarker in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AXL overexpression was detected in almost 13% of patients at baseline before treatment, at 16% after the first cycle of therapy..." | Speaker said: "our results indicate that longitudinal monitoring ... could be a precious tool for the assessment of osimertinib efficacy and early therapeutic intervention with AXL inhibitors."

talk_title: Circulating Tumor Cell‑Informed Pipeline for Rapid Personalization of Synergistic Cytokine‑Armed CAR‑NK Cell Therapy in Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma

talk_title: ctDNA Clearance Dynamics in MSI‑high Colorectal Cancer Treated with Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors

talk_title: CUPIA 2: a collaborative multi-agent large language model framework for diagnosis of cancer of unknown primary
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we put all the mutation results like the KRAS, TP53, two gene losses, all to CUPIA 2 return as a prediction" | Speaker said: "we know CUP issue, but actually I would argue more than CUP" | Speaker said: "we have a limitation: small cell lung cancer and endometrial, the F1 score is lower" | Speaker said: "there is a 15-point gap between internal and MSK validation, which is kind of expected" | Speaker said: "we put a single KRAS G12C to any prediction, is not possible to give a unique result anyway"

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the study did not meet its primary endpoint of OS benefit in high TMB group" | Speaker said: "AI‑derived fibroblast phenotype significantly separated patients in both IO and chemo arms" | Speaker said: "adding H&E‑based features to a multivariate Cox model made the separation significant" | Speaker said: "these features performed competitively or better than PD‑L1, TMB, age, gender"

talk_title: Clinical successes and failures of macrophage‑targeted therapies and IL‑4/IL‑1 blockade strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have more failures than successes so far" | Speaker admitted: "CCR2/5 inhibitor plus nivolumab did not improve pathologic responses" | Speaker noted: "CD47 blockade showed hemolysis and development was halted"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized that macrophage depletion must avoid killing 'good' macrophages, yet many trials (CSF1R, CCR2/5) indiscriminately reduced both suppressive and beneficial myeloid cells, contradicting the stated precision goal

talk_title: Advances and Challenges in CAR T Cell Engineering
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We know that we can armor against negative signals." | Speaker said: "I'm not telling you if that's good or bad. The jury is still out, but it certainly does happen." | Speaker said: "We are giving futile therapy to some of our patients that we know that their CAR T cells are not going to do anything against that leukemia or that lymphoma."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that Cilta‑cel‑like CAR self‑clusters and secretes cytokines in vitro (observation) yet still presented it as the gold‑standard comparator for BCMA targeting (contradiction). | Speaker described extensive epigenetic programming to make exhaustion‑proof CAR T cells (observation) while also acknowledging that many patients receive futile therapy due to inherent CAR resistance (contradiction).

crispro_top_description: Identify patients with pre‑existing antigen loss or CAR self‑association signatures to select those who will benefit from engineered CARs.

talk_title: Innate Cell Product Engineering: Gamma‑Delta T Cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you have a robust recovery in gamma delta cells after transplant, you have a higher chance of survival." | Speaker said: "Not all donors are equal, so the use of an allogeneic gamma delta T cell is a major goal, but choosing the donor randomly is a poor strategy." | Speaker said: "We lose CD16 expression when we expand with TGF‑beta, but genetic engineering will replace the ADCC component."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that NK cells dominate killing in mixed cultures (observation) yet emphasized gamma‑delta cells as the primary therapeutic engine (contradiction). | Speaker demonstrated that TGF‑beta improves central memory phenotype (observation) while also stating that CD16‑mediated ADCC is lost, which is critical for antibody‑dependent killing (contradiction).
mechanistic_blindspot: Donor variability and loss of CD16‑mediated ADCC are not fully mitigated in current expansion protocols.
crispro_top_description: Develop predictive markers (e.g., exercise‑induced gene signatures) to select high‑expanding donors.

talk_title: In‑vivo Lentiviral Vector CAR Delivery (VivoVEC) Platform
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are able to manufacture that in a suspension‑based manufacturing process." | Speaker said: "We saw rapid and complete B cell depletion, concomitant to the peak CAR T cell expansion around day 7‑10." | Speaker said: "The risk of a resultant T cell malignancy is extremely low. Far lower, actually, than ex‑vivo manufactured CAR T cells."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust CAR T expansion and B‑cell depletion (observation) yet emphasized that the platform does not require lymphodepletion, which contradicts the known need for T‑cell activation to achieve efficient transduction. | Speaker highlighted low immunogenicity of lentiviral vectors (observation) while reporting a strong anti‑CAR antibody response that drives B‑cell recovery (contradiction).
mechanistic_blindspot: Anti‑CAR immune response against murine scFv and human hinge/endodomain leads to B‑cell recovery.
crispro_top_description: Leverage CrisPRO to design next‑generation scFv domains with reduced immunogenicity for the MDF.

talk_title: Spatial Omics Platforms and Analytical Challenges
rhetorical_signals: I think a lot of people dive right in because they're commercially available. | There are a lot of bottlenecks and things that I think people need to consider. | I think if you are looking for tools and the latest and greatest, I think you should also consider a lot of the work that's on bioRxiv.

talk_title: Targeting Telomeres in Cancer and Aging
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we think that this is ..." | Speaker said: "we don't fully understand that" | Speaker said: "we have not demonstrated it. It's just a hypothesis"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that telomerase gene therapy did not increase tumor number in K‑RAS driven mouse lung cancer, then concluded that telomerase activation is safe for humans despite acknowledging species differences

talk_title: Dendritic cell metabolism and therapeutic rewiring
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we can improve their metabolic and mitochondria fitness" | Speaker said: "adoptive transfer of CDC1 with better mitochondria fitness shows greatly enhanced ability to slow down tumor growth" | Speaker said: "we have not looked at the metastatic tumor system yet"
crispro_top_description: Identify and isolate mitochondrial‑high conventional dendritic cells (cDC1) in human tumors to enable adoptive DC therapy.

talk_title: BACH1‑driven transcriptional plasticity and immune evasion in triple‑negative breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "BACH1 knockout can really increase amount of CD8 T cell infiltrate into a tumor" | Speaker said: "BACH1 activity really predicts response rate in TNBC in a pembrolizumab and chemo arm" | Speaker said: "we have not looked at the metastatic tumor system yet"
crispro_top_description: Target BACH1‑driven transcriptional program to reverse T‑cell exclusion and improve checkpoint inhibitor response in TNBC.

talk_title: Temporal dynamics of PD‑1 blockade and targeting CD22 to rescue exhausted T cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "long‑term loss of PD‑1 signals is not always an advantage for these cells" | Speaker said: "a drug holiday allows for sufficient rest for these cells to mount a new reinvigoration response" | Speaker said: "CD22 is a potent negative regulatory molecule on progenitor exhausted T cells"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that continuous PD‑1 blockade yields a peak of T cell reinvigoration then returns to baseline, yet concluded that "long‑term loss of PD‑1 signals is not always an advantage" – contradictory to the notion that sustained blockade should maintain benefit
mechanistic_blindspot: Continuous PD‑1 blockade erodes progenitor‑exhausted (Tpex) cells and skews differentiation toward terminally exhausted cells

nct_numbers: NCT02834013 (PD‑1 continuous dosing in melanoma); NCT04571453 (bispecific PD‑1/CD22 trial, if existent)

talk_title: Mechanisms of resistance to direct RAS inhibitors and approaches for overcoming resistance
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have many inhibitors of the oncogenes, over 100 kinase inhibitors, new KRAS inhibitors that I'll mention a little bit of" | Speaker said: "we see a single example of a complete response in this waterfall plot, but then you notice a patient responded, but then had progressive disease and relapsed" | Speaker said: "the promise of it, but getting that to play out correctly, of course, is a big challenge"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that allele‑specific KRAS G12C inhibitors spare T cells (observation) but concluded that “selective killing of even a subpopulation will alert the immune system” without providing data that T‑cell function is truly preserved (contradiction)
mechanistic_blindspot: Emergence of resistant subclones (e.g., KRAS G12D) that share neoantigens but are not targeted by G12C‑selective drugs

talk_title: Intrinsic mechanisms of resistance to RAS inhibitors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have intrinsic resistance due to KEAP1, SMARCA4, and CDKN2N alterations" | Speaker said: "acquired alterations in cis at residues 95, 96, 68 impair drug binding" | Speaker said: "adaptive resistance involves transcriptional rewiring and wild‑type RAS activation"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed preclinical data that SOS1 or MEK combinations dramatically improve tumor control (observation) but concluded that “we still need many trials to test all these combos” without presenting a clear prioritization framework (contradiction)
mechanistic_blindspot: Co‑occurring KEAP1, SMARCA4, CDKN2N alterations that blunt response to monotherapy
crispro_top_description: Develop CrisPRO‑based predictive models to identify patients with KEAP1/SMARCA4/CDKN2N co‑mutations who may need upfront combination therapy

talk_title: Hippo‑YAP/TAZ pathway as a driver of adaptive resistance and immune evasion
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "adaptive activation of Hippo‑YAP/TAZ pathway can be a mechanism of resistance" | Speaker said: "FAK inhibition combined with avotamgenib showed synergistic tumor reduction" | Speaker said: "RND3‑RhoE loop links ERK inhibition to YAP activation"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that avotamgenib strongly suppresses ERK re‑activation (observation) but also admitted that “MEK inhibitors still cause toxicity and limited durability” (contradiction) | Speaker demonstrated robust preclinical synergy of FAK + TED inhibition (observation) yet concluded that “we still need biomarkers to know when to use these combos” without presenting candidate biomarkers (contradiction)
mechanistic_blindspot: Rapid up‑regulation of FAK and YAP target genes after ERK inhibition via RND3 repression
crispro_top_description: Use CrisPRO to predict YAP/TAZ target gene up‑regulation after MEK inhibition and pre‑emptively add FAK/TED inhibitors

talk_title: Mechanobiology of glioblastoma invasion using hyaluronic acid-based biomaterials
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we're under no illusion whatsoever that we can recapitulate all of the nuances of a patient tumor with this very simple in vitro system" | Speaker said: "we don't know what induces hyaluronidase secretion"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that leader cells secrete HYAL2 to digest HA and facilitate invasion, then concluded that "we are still not sure what triggers hyaluronidase secretion" – contradiction between mechanistic insight and lack of upstream trigger

talk_title: Stiffness-driven EMT via TYK2–Twist signaling in triple‑negative breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't know what the sensor is" | Speaker said: "We have no phenotype when we block JAK1/2 or interferon signaling, only TYK2 inhibition shows effect"

talk_title: One Gene at a Time: Metabolic Adaptations Driving Metastasis

talk_title: Metabolic Changes Associated with Senescence in CAFs Regulate Fibrosis in Pancreatic Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we haven't looked at that yet" regarding AKG release | Speaker said: "we are currently targeting IDH2/IDH3"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed increased α‑KG in senescent CAFs but concluded that targeting IDH2/3 will reduce collagen without demonstrating that α‑KG is the limiting substrate for hydroxylases
mechanistic_blindspot: Accumulation of α‑ketoglutarate in senescent CAFs may limit prolyl hydroxylase activity if not supplied, but the pathway's dependency is not yet proven
crispro_top_description: Develop a senescent‑CAF metabolic signature (high α‑KG, PKM2/1 ratio) to identify PDAC patients who may benefit from IDH inhibition

talk_title: Targeting Metabolism in BRCA Deficient Tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we observed an additive effect" when combining mitapivat with PARP inhibitors | Speaker said: "we haven't done the resistant models yet"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PKM2 activation causes mitochondrial dysfunction yet concluded that the drug is well‑tolerated with no weight loss in mice
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of PKM1 upregulation in wildtype limits drug sensitivity
crispro_top_description: Develop a PKM1‑high signature to select BRCA‑mutant patients likely to respond to PKM activators

talk_title: Stromal microenvironment as a predictor of bone metastasis in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: One caveat is that clinical trials targeting the stromal component have not been very successful | we think very strongly that periostin is associated both with neuroendocrine differentiation and organotropism
mechanistic_blindspot: Neglect of fibroblast heterogeneity and AR‑loss driven periostin signaling
crispro_top_description: Periostin‑rich stromal signature could be used to stratify patients for bone‑metastasis risk and to select trials of bone‑targeted therapies.

talk_title: Metabolic coding of organotropism in breast cancer metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Metastatic success is not about brute force. It is all the growth signaling nutrient requirements that are markedly different in the different metastatic niches | We were quite keen to show that bone metastatic cancer cells are very sensitive to glutamine dependency
mechanistic_blindspot: Redundant glutamine transporters limit efficacy of single transporter inhibition
crispro_top_description: Targeting GLS1 activity (e.g., with CBA39) could overcome glutamine addiction of bone‑metastatic breast cancer cells.
cited_competitors: Vander Heiden lab; Sarah Fenn lab

talk_title: Circulating tumor cell heterogeneity and the role of TMPO in metastasis
rhetorical_signals: TMPO is a nuclear membrane‑associated protein that allows cells to survive stress in circulation | We think TMPO is allowing the cells to survive the stress and to find their home at the metastatic site
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of therapeutic agents that specifically inhibit TMPO‑driven hypoxia‑oxphos switch
crispro_top_description: TMPO expression in CTCs could serve as a prognostic biomarker for metastatic risk and bone tropism in prostate cancer.
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing nuclear envelope targeting therapeutics

institution: Ghent University and VIB, Flemish Institute for Biotechnology
talk_title: Colibactin‑driven genotoxicity depends on epithelial adhesion
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I wouldn't advise you to take it maybe" referring to E. coli Nissle as a probiotic | Speaker said: "It's quite alarming also because this is a strain that is sold as a probiotic in Europe under the name Mutaflor" | Speaker said: "We still haven't resolved how colibactin migrates to the nucleus"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that both pks+ strains (11G5 and Nissle) produce colibactin, yet only 11G5 exacerbates CRC, but concluded that colibactin production alone is sufficient for tumor promotion
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of functional redundancy in bacterial adhesins; only FimH/FimLH variants with high‑affinity binding drive genotoxicity
crispro_top_description: Targeting FimH/FimLH adhesin interaction could block colibactin delivery to epithelial cells

institution: Harvard Chan School of Public Health
talk_title: Dietary sulfur amino acids, Mucispirillum schaedleri and NKT‑driven anti‑tumor immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't fully understand why the mucus thickens with the high sulfur amino acid diet" | Speaker said: "The phospholipids from M. schaedleri stimulate NKT cells independent of CD1d" | Speaker said: "We have no statistical significance for increased granzyme B, but we still highlight the trend"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that high sulfur amino acid diet thickens mucus (a protective barrier) yet concluded that this diet is beneficial for anti‑tumor immunity without fully explaining the paradox
mechanistic_blindspot: Unclear why mucus layer thickens; the exact host pathway linking diet to mucus production is not defined
crispro_top_description: Combine FimH blockade (from Verbeke) with high‑sulfur amino acid diet to synergistically reduce CRC by limiting both bacterial adhesion and enhancing NKT‑CDC1 immunity
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing NKT‑targeted immunotherapies; Food‑flavoring manufacturers of ethyl acetoacetate

institution: University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
talk_title: Intratumoral bacteria reprogram tumor cell states and therapy response
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We did not see increased proliferation; instead we observed cell‑cycle arrest" | Speaker said: "Most bacterial signals in colorectal tumors are extracellular" | Speaker said: "We cannot yet distinguish correlation from causation in necrotic regions"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that Fusobacterium exposure reduces Ki‑67 proliferation in patient tumors, yet many prior studies claim Fusobacterium drives hyper‑proliferation
mechanistic_blindspot: Induction of reversible G0/G1 quiescence in tumor cells, rendering antimetabolite chemotherapy ineffective
crispro_top_description: Develop a computational platform to integrate spatial microbial load, host transcriptomics, and therapy response, outperforming current spatial assays
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing microbiome‑targeted antibiotics for oncology

talk_title: Neuroinflammatory memory in gastric tumorigenesis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Even after a successful eradication, gastric cancer still occurred in about 2.6% of treated individual." | Speaker said: "The memory effect persists for at least six months after the initial injury." | Speaker said: "IL-13 is the dominant signal that drives and stabilizes inflammatory memory."

talk_title: T cell exhaustion after steatosis in the tumor microenvironment
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "ICF therapy, both monotherapy and combination therapy, are not effective in MASLD-associated HCC." | Speaker said: "CXCL9 knockout mice showed a significant reduced liver cancer development." | Speaker said: "Granzyme K knockout mice shows the significantly reduced number of its development."
