crispro_top_description: Target IL‑13 signaling in ILC2‑neuron axis to prevent inflammatory memory‑driven tumor promotion.

talk_title: Stress remodeling of enteric neural system in colitis to cancer transition
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Physical restraint stress can lead to much higher levels of corticosterone, which means that these mice are under stress." | Speaker said: "Depleting glia cells at the very early stage could significantly reduce stress‑induced tumorigenesis." | Speaker said: "Stressed glia cells activate the GR pathways and trigger transcriptional changes in stem cells."
crispro_top_description: Combine glucocorticoid receptor antagonists with CRC prevention strategies to block stress‑induced glial activation.

talk_title: Chronic sleep deprivation promotes colon cancer via microbiota dysbiosis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "SD mice were significantly more susceptible to tumor volume compared to the non‑SD counterparts." | Speaker said: "BMAL1 was significantly decreased in the SD mice compared to the non‑SD counterparts." | Speaker said: "SD mice had a significant decrease in CD27 and interferon gamma in tumor mRNA levels."
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of microbiota‑targeted adjuvant to restore chemosensitivity
crispro_top_description: Identify patients with circadian disruption (e.g., low BMAL1) as poor responders to 5‑FU.

talk_title: Obesity‑induced changes in adipose tissue in ovarian cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "High BMI preadipocytes had a significant increase in growth relative to injections of cancer cells alone." | Speaker said: "IGF secretion was increased between both normal and high BMI preadipocytes." | Speaker said: "High BMI preadipocyte group showed a decrease in the probability of survival."
crispro_top_description: Repurpose IGF‑1R inhibitors for obesity‑driven ovarian cancer metastasis.

talk_title: Immunotherapy resistance to bone metastasis mediated by myeloid‑driven mechanisms
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "A distinct mature neutrophil with elevated expression of IL1B became significantly enriched in tumor bone biopsies." | Speaker said: "IL‑1 receptor inhibition combined with checkpoint blockade significantly reduced tumor growth in intraosseous prostate cancer." | Speaker said: "Elevated target neutrophil signature scores are highly associated with reduced survival."
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of patient selection based on IL1B‑high neutrophil signature
crispro_top_description: Develop a CrisPRO‑derived IL1B‑high neutrophil signature to select patients for IL‑1R antagonist + ICB therapy.

talk_title: The Genomic Landscape of Likely Human Pre-cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the vast majority of pre-cancers will never progress to cancer" | Speaker said: "we need better clinical data to be able to answer that specific question"
crispro_top_description: Need prospective biomarker‑driven validation to separate indolent from high‑risk pre‑cancers using the genomic risk score.

talk_title: Homologous recombination deficiency synthesise breast cancer to therapeutic targeting of ectonucleotidase ENPP1
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the combination, although it was a good idea, we don't see any effect in the patients over multiple clinical trials" | Speaker said: "we found this antibody ... was equal to the knocking out the gene entirely in tumors"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ENPP1 knockout leads to tumor rejection in ~50% of mice, then concluded that ENPP1 is a promising single‑agent therapeutic target
mechanistic_blindspot: ENPP1 blockade does not affect primary tumor growth without additional immune activation
crispro_top_description: Combine ENPP1 inhibition with PARP inhibitor (niraparib) and anti‑PD‑1 to achieve synergistic tumor control.

talk_title: Mechanisms of Tumor Dormancy Through Immune Niche Formation and Its Impact on Immune Therapeutic Relapse
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we used inducible S2GP system ... the total lesion number was not dramatically changed early on, which is inconsistent with the cellular dormancy rather than failure of tumor seeding" | Speaker said: "we did not find any evidence for transdifferentiation"
crispro_top_description: Target CD200‑CD200R axis to eliminate dormant lesions that evade NK‑cell killing.

institution: University of Michigan / Shanghai Institute of Chemistry
talk_title: Ligandable PNT domain establishes ERG as a directable, targetable oncogenic driver in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "PBIDE1 is a weak but very selective" | Speaker said: "we have not yet assessed homology with other ETS family members beyond FLI1"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that PBIDE1 stabilizes ERG in thermal shift assays, yet concluded that the compound is sufficient to suppress tumor growth despite its weak affinity
mechanistic_blindspot: Low binding affinity (28 µM) limits functional inhibition of ERG transcriptional program
crispro_top_description: Improve affinity and drug‑likeness of PNT binders using CrisPRO‑guided fragment merging.

talk_title: A novel brain‑penetrant BRAF inhibitor for BRAF mutant gliomas
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "HSK42360 demonstrated favorably with reported dabrafenib plus trametinib results" | Speaker said: "we did not observe any patients reach the response rate" (in resistant cohort)
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited drug exposure may underlie lack of responses in resistant cohorts
crispro_top_description: Design a randomized phase II trial with a biomarker‑stratified arm to compare HSK42360 versus dabrafenib+trametinib in BRAF‑mutant glioma.

talk_title: Active RAS inhibition intercepts pancreas cancer in mice
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you start and you eliminate pan‑en, preventing tumors from forming, you can actually extend overall survival by over a year" | Speaker said: "We have not done macrophage or fibroblast depletion experiments yet"
crispro_top_description: Apply KRAS interception strategy to high‑risk human cohorts (e.g., germline predisposition, pancreatic cysts).

talk_title: Phase I/II trial of nab‑paclitaxel and gemcitabine with deruxnaratib in first‑line metastatic pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We were unable to calculate median PFS and OS because there were not enough events yet" | Speaker said: "The combination demonstrated compelling initial evidence of activity"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high ORR (58%) yet acknowledged that median PFS/OS could not be calculated due to insufficient events, creating uncertainty about durability
mechanistic_blindspot: High incidence (73%) of grade ≥ 3 adverse events driven by overlapping rash and chemotherapy toxicities
crispro_top_description: Incorporate a biomarker‑selected arm (KRAS G12D vs other) to enrich for patients most likely to benefit.

talk_title: Gapless But Not Global: Ancestry-Enriched Functional Sequence Still Missing from the Human Reference Genomes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Just because the reference is gapless does not mean it's representative of your given population." | Speaker said: "If you have missing sequence, you might miss biology that's pertinent to making a clinical decision."
crispro_top_description: Current clinical pipelines use GRCh38 or hg19, missing ~300 Mb of ancestry-enriched sequence that can affect somatic variant calling and neoantigen prediction in African ancestry patients.

talk_title: Epigenetics, ancestry, and cancer disparity: when nature might be nurture
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I don't have data to support these speculations." | Speaker said: "We are speculating that epigenetics could be a link between group specific environmental exposures and genetically influenced pathophysiological states."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PAQR6 is up‑regulated and hypomethylated in African ancestry breast cancer, then concluded that epigenetics 'has a greater contribution to cancer survival disparities than group specific genetic variants' without functional proof.
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of direct functional validation of PAQR6 methylation effects on tumor biology
crispro_top_description: PAQR6 hypomethylation and over‑expression in African ancestry breast cancer may represent a targetable epigenetic driver.

talk_title: Translating genomic equity: building infrastructure for global precision oncology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are starting to resolve an important question. Prostate tumors have been immunologically cold. They have not been able to benefit from immune therapy. Hopefully, we can convince you today that we see robust numbers of immune cells..." | Speaker said: "We think these patients to start to benefit from the vast knowledge that we have of many types of immunotherapy, if we can start to pinpoint those and revisit this, particularly for patients of African ancestry."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented extensive single‑cell and spatial data showing abundant immune infiltration in African‑ancestry prostate tumors, then concluded that prostate cancer is generally immunologically cold without acknowledging the ancestry‑specific reversal.
failing_target: Current immune checkpoint monotherapy in African‑ancestry prostate cancer
mechanistic_blindspot: High CD8+ TEM cells co‑expressing LAG‑3 indicate T‑cell exhaustion not addressed by PD‑1/PD‑L1 blockade alone
crispro_top_description: Identify LAG‑3 blockade as a rational partner to existing checkpoint inhibitors for African‑ancestry prostate cancer patients with exhausted CD8+ TEM cells.

institution: University of South Alabama (moving to Oklahoma University)
talk_title: Neuronal mitochondria transfer drives breast cancer metastasis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we saw a drastic decrease on metastasis" | Speaker said: "we saw a 35% decrease on the transfer between the mouse to the tumor by 35%" | Speaker said: "some may argue that 35% is not a majority, but think about the quantity of neurons in a cancer like that"
mechanistic_blindspot: Low proportion of tumor cells receiving mitochondria (10‑15% in primary, 5‑6% in primary, 5‑6% in primary but only 35% reduction when nerves removed) limits therapeutic impact
crispro_top_description: Identify patients with high nerve density or mitochondrial content as a biomarker for susceptibility to mitochondrial transfer‑targeted therapies.

talk_title: Tumor‑derived mitochondrial transfer to immune cells impairs anti‑tumor immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "40% of human T cells have mitochondrial DNA mutation, many of which shared with cancer cell" | Speaker said: "Mutated mitochondria transfer impairs memory formation and activation" | Speaker said: "Patients with mitochondrial DNA mutated tumors have poor prognosis by ICI"
failing_target: Tumor‑derived mutant mitochondria transfer to T cells
mechanistic_blindspot: USP30‑mediated mitophagy resistance allows dysfunctional mitochondria to persist in T cells
crispro_top_description: Develop inhibitors of USP30 or enhance mitophagy in T cells to prevent accumulation of mutant mitochondria.

talk_title: Leveraging mitochondrial transfer to super‑charge adoptive T‑cell therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We observed a transfer rate about of 10%" | Speaker said: "Mito‑positive cells showed higher basal respiration and spare capacity" | Speaker said: "Mito‑positive cells induced prolonged tumor control and survival"
mechanistic_blindspot: Dependence on talin‑2 and other adhesion molecules; residual transfer likely via extracellular vesicles not yet characterized
crispro_top_description: Demonstrate that ex‑vivo mitochondrial augmentation outperforms pharmacologic PGC‑1α activation for TIL metabolic fitness.
companies_to_monitor: Cell therapy platforms exploring metabolic augmentation

institution: NCI Center for Biomedical Informatics and IT
talk_title: AI Augmentation for Clinical Trial Operations
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "dropping AI into a broken or unsupported environment doesn't fix the problem. It just automates the chaos." | Speaker said: "We have to build the connective tissue around that, such as governance standards, workflows, and human checkpoint."

institution: NCI Center for Biomedical Informatics and IT
talk_title: AI‑Generated Consent Documents and Multilingual Translation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "This is AI‑generated, and this is ready for human review and refinement. It is not intended to go directly to the IRB for approval." | Speaker said: "We can cut translation time in half, about two and a half days, with an AI‑translated document that the human goes in and compares to the English version."
crispro_top_description: Need for AI tools that can map protocol eligibility criteria to common data elements to enable interoperable patient matching.

institution: National Cancer Institute, Center for Immuno‑Oncology
talk_title: Why AI Matters for Patients and Clinical Teams
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The combination of high cognitive load, administrative overhead, and prolonged timeline has real consequences. This limits the number of trials that can open, it slows accrual, and it also constrains the pace of clinical research progress overall." | Speaker said: "We need to avoid unnecessary complexity so that AI tools can safely, reliably move the needle."
crispro_top_description: Opportunity to integrate CrisPRO decision‑support with AI tools that reduce clinician cognitive load in trial enrollment and documentation.

institution: FDA Division of Hematology, Oncology, Toxicology (DHOT)
talk_title: Overview of Streamlined Non‑clinical Development and Weight‑of‑Evidence Approaches
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Importantly, there is no evidence that the reduction in animal testing has compromised patient safety." | Speaker said: "Weight‑of‑evidence risk assessments are powerful tools to reduce certain types of animal studies." | Speaker said: "The oncology community has been a leader in reducing animal testing, and this is because of the unique considerations of oncology drug development."
crispro_top_description: FDA highlights that weight‑of‑evidence approaches can replace three‑month GLP toxicology studies for CD3 bispecifics and ADCs, indicating a need for computational tools to integrate diverse data streams and predict chronic toxicity without animal studies.

institution: FDA Division of Hematology, Oncology, Toxicology (DHOT)
talk_title: Incorporating New Approach Methodologies (NAMs) in Oncology Drug Development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There is no harmonized global definition of NAMs, and the recent FDA roadmap discusses that NAMs can be 3D cell cultures, organoids, organ‑on‑chip, in silico, including AI and computational tox, and in chemical or omics, for example, protein assays." | Speaker said: "Only 1% of commercial IND submissions per year contain hiPSC‑CM studies, indicating a gap between NAM availability and adoption." | Speaker said: "Weight‑of‑evidence approaches can incorporate NAMs to replace animal studies, but the acceptance is still limited to well‑characterized targets and class‑specific toxicities."
crispro_top_description: Only ~1% of INDs include hiPSC‑CM NAMs, suggesting an opportunity for CrisPRO to develop predictive biomarkers that justify broader NAM adoption in oncology INDs.

institution: FDA Division of Hematology, Oncology, Toxicology (DHOT) – Bioinformatics Unit
talk_title: A Glimpse into the Future in Bioinformatics
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have a database of over 8,000 commercial INDs in oncology, cataloguing mechanisms, targets, and classes by product." | Speaker said: "We are considering hypothetical stepwise approaches that could replace three‑month non‑rodent studies with weight‑of‑evidence assessments." | Speaker said: "The increase in oncology IND submissions suggests a potential rise in animal use unless alternative designs are adopted."
crispro_top_description: Speaker proposes hypothetical designs that replace non‑rodent three‑month studies with weight‑of‑evidence; CrisPRO can provide quantitative risk models to support such designs and justify omission of animal data.

talk_title: Bone Repair External State Program Drives Cancer Cell Dedifferentiation and Immune Invasion in Melanoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see that anti-inflammatory hub is the most predictive of a higher neural crest trajectory" | Speaker said: "we see that postin, which is this ECM ligand, when we put it on cancer cells, we see the cancer cells dedifferentiate"
crispro_top_description: Target the SPP1/POSTN tissue repair ligand program that drives melanoma dedifferentiation and immune evasion.

talk_title: Tumor-Specific Antibodies Elicited by Engineered Bacteria Promote Bladder Cancer Immunotherapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "there was no difference as compared to the PBS control group or the bacteria empty vector" | Speaker said: "we improved the survival of those mice by two times"
failing_target: Engineered E. coli Nissle delivering CXCL13
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited induction of tertiary lymphoid structures in bladder wall
crispro_top_description: Combine engineered bacteria with checkpoint blockade to enhance TLS formation and antibody responses.

talk_title: Integrating single-cell and spatial transcriptomics with mouse models of melanoma and breast cancer to understand how tumor‑associated macrophages suppress antitumor immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "knockout of host‑derived GPNMB leads to tumor control" | Speaker said: "absence of GPNMB from the host led to tumor control, while bone‑marrow deletion did not"
mechanistic_blindspot: GPNMB expression on non‑bone‑marrow‑derived tissue‑resident macrophages maintains immunosuppressive TME
crispro_top_description: Develop small‑molecule or antibody inhibitors of GPNMB to recapitulate knockout phenotype.

talk_title: Vaccine Stimulation of Bystander T cells in the Lymph Node Unexpectedly Promotes Tumor‑Specific Host Immunity to Regress Solid Tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If we deplete the CAR T cell 24 h after vaccination we almost immediately lose tumor control" | Speaker said: "Blocking interferon‑γ almost completely loss of therapeutic efficacy"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed rapid tumor regression within 24 h after vaccine but concluded that the effect is mediated by host CD8 T cells, not the CAR T cells, despite the CAR T cells being tumor‑irrelevant
crispro_top_description: Identify patients with sufficient vaccine‑responsive T‑cell precursors to benefit from bystander CAR T boosting.
companies_to_monitor: Firms developing lipid‑based vaccine delivery systems

talk_title: IL‑18‑secreting CAR T cells uncover divergent immune mechanisms of antigen‑heterogeneous solid tumor clearance
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "IL‑18 CARs outperformed controls despite lower CAR persistence" | Speaker said: "Neutrophil depletion had no impact on tumor control"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker observed low endogenous CD8 tumor‑reactive ELISpot responses yet concluded that IL‑18 CARs mediate tumor clearance via innate myeloid activation
crispro_top_description: Target IL‑18‑driven myeloid activation pathways to enhance CAR T efficacy in heterogeneous tumors.
companies_to_monitor: CAR T developers incorporating cytokine payloads

talk_title: Emergency Myelopoiesis Drives Immunosuppressive Monocyte Programming in Lymphoma Associated Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "MS1 monocytes were expanded in secondary HLH patients" | Speaker said: "IL‑6 and IL‑10 plasma levels correlated with MS1 score"
mechanistic_blindspot: Emergency myelopoiesis imprinting immunosuppressive phenotype at GMP stage
crispro_top_description: Use CrisPRO to model emergency myelopoiesis and predict agents that disrupt MS1 programming.
companies_to_monitor: Pharma developing cytokine blockade for HLH

talk_title: Gut Microeukaryotes, Endogenous Viruses, and Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "At the very least, it may indicate that it has a non‑pathogenic role given how prevalent it is" | Speaker said: "We think that this may have implications for primary and secondary prevention of obesity‑mediated cancers"
crispro_top_description: Blastocystis prevalence correlates with plant‑based diet, favorable cardiometabolic markers, and lower colorectal cancer odds, suggesting a potential non‑invasive biomarker for cancer risk stratification.

talk_title: Population‑scale EBV persistence and disease risk
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are essentially identifying a segment of the population that's in this right‑hand tail, that they have very high levels of persistent EBV" | Speaker said: "These 10% of people are special in the way that they processed or essentially failed to process the virus"
crispro_top_description: Genetic loci linked to EBV persistence (e.g., antigen presentation genes) could be targeted to enhance viral clearance.

talk_title: The cancer mycobiome: truth, myth, and current data
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Candida albicans is mucosal‑associated and drives IL‑17/IL‑1 responses that can be genotoxic via candidalysin" | Speaker said: "Fluconazole cleared Candida from both mouth and gut, whereas oral nystatin only cleared the mouth"
crispro_top_description: Systemic fluconazole reduces gut Candida and restores beneficial butyrate‑producing bacteria, suggesting a combination of antifungal and microbiome‑modulating therapy for colorectal cancer.

talk_title: LINE1 as a pan‑cancer biomarker and ultra‑sensitive detection platform
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't know the kinetics of some of this" | Speaker said: "we have no evidence that there is a normal level of OR1 in the blood" | Speaker said: "we don't know why LINE‑1 is low in brain tumors"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high specificity of ORF1P in cancer tissue but then admitted "we don't know what kind of tissue the LINE‑1 is coming from" in blood, contradicting the claim of a useful early‑detection biomarker
failing_target: LINE1 ORF1 protein blood assay (first‑generation and second‑generation ELISA/Mosaic)
mechanistic_blindspot: Low detection sensitivity in gastro‑esophageal (≈30%) and brain/kidney cancers; assay does not capture tissue of origin
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could provide computational deconvolution of ORF1P signal to infer tissue of origin using multi‑omics integration

institution: University of Pennsylvania / CRUK Grand Challenge
talk_title: Humoral immunity and auto‑antibody atlases for cancer early detection
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't know why some viral antibodies correlate with poor prognosis" | Speaker said: "we have very little data on how auto‑antibodies arise in cancer" | Speaker said: "we don't yet know whether the viral exposure signature is causal or correlative"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented data that auto‑antibodies improve AUC when combined, yet repeatedly noted that single‑marker performance is poor, creating tension between the promise of a biomarker panel and its current limited predictive power
mechanistic_blindspot: Low specificity; many antibodies are present in healthy or autoimmune cohorts, leading to modest AUCs
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can integrate auto‑antibody signatures with ctDNA and proteomics to create a multi‑modal risk score
cited_competitors: Grail (Galleri); Early Detection Research Network (NCI); PhIP‑seq (VirScan) platforms
companies_to_monitor: Grail; Illumina (for PhIP‑seq); Thermo Fisher (protein arrays)

talk_title: Multi-Cancer Early Detection: Where Are We and Where Do We Want to Go, to Get To?
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "In February, Grail announced that the NHS Galleri, the only randomized controlled trial of a multi-cancer screening test with a clinical endpoint, failed to achieve its primary endpoint, a statistically significant reduction in stage three and four cancers three years after the last participant was enrolled." | Speaker said: "The Galleri trial reported a favorable trend in reducing stage four diagnoses in high mortality cancers, and a suggestion that a reduction in stage four cancers increased with longer follow‑up." | Speaker said: "The positive predicted value of a positive Galleri test was 62%, substantially higher than with any existing screening program or indeed most symptoms." | Speaker said: "Tests based on cell‑free DNA may never be able to detect most stage one cancers and certainly not intraepithelial neoplasia and adenomas."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high specificity (≥96.4%) and a PPV of 62% for Galleri, then concluded that the test’s limited sensitivity for early‑stage tumors makes it unlikely to improve mortality – contradictory emphasis on strong performance metrics versus weak clinical impact.
mechanistic_blindspot: Limit of detection at ~0.1% tumor fraction prevents detection of early‑stage, low‑shedding tumors
crispro_top_description: Develop computational models to predict low‑shedding tumors and integrate additional biomarkers (e.g., proteins, microRNA) to overcome the 0.1% tumor fraction detection limit.
cited_competitors: Grail; Guardant Health Shield; Exact Sciences

talk_title: Navigating from Hype to Hope in Multi-Cancer Early Detection
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Traditional screening trials were to mortality. The gallery trial is precedent setting. It's going to look at the reduction in late‑stage incidents." | Speaker said: "We need to understand that down‑staging studies may feed the hype cycle if we don't ask the right questions." | Speaker said: "The early stage opportunity for high‑grade serious cancers was less than four months, making down‑staging unlikely to translate into mortality benefit."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that high diagnostic performance (e.g., CA125/ROCA) does not translate into mortality benefit, yet the field continues to promote such biomarkers as viable screening tools.
mechanistic_blindspot: Assumes uniform correlation between stage reduction and mortality across cancer types without accounting for variable sojourn times
crispro_top_description: Apply CrisPRO’s multi‑omic integration to identify high‑risk subpopulations where early‑stage sojourn time is longer, improving trial enrichment.

talk_title: Stage as an Endpoint in Cancer Screening Trials
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There have been three meta‑analyses looking at stage tracking with a reduction in advanced stage disease and subsequent outcome with mortality." | Speaker said: "We could drop prevalent cases from both screen and control arms to get a more powerful comparison for incidence rounds of screening." | Speaker said: "The data for many historic trials is no longer accessible, making individual patient meta‑analysis difficult."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker advocated for dropping prevalent cases to improve statistical power, yet also noted that many historic trial datasets are unavailable, limiting the feasibility of such re‑analyses.
failing_target: Use of stage reduction as surrogate endpoint
mechanistic_blindspot: Does not account for lead‑time bias and variable natural history across cancer types
crispro_top_description: Implement prevalence‑adjusted analysis pipelines using CrisPRO to retrospectively re‑evaluate existing trial data for stage‑specific outcomes.

talk_title: NCI Director's Address
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I want to make it clear, although this may have started as an NCI idea, it is not a purely NCI project." | Speaker said: "I am concerned that I don't think a lot of America understands what the National Cancer Institute does..." | Speaker said: "I would say the damage was on the basis of misinformation and incorrect information about how some of these decisions were made..." | Speaker said: "I think we will continue to be in a situation where nine out of 10 grants are not going to get funded, but that's not new."
crispro_top_description: Dr. Lattai highlighted the need to develop and validate functional precision medicine assays using patient tumor tissue, noting logistical challenges such as tissue preservation and lack of standardized workflows.

talk_title: Innovative Molecular Analysis Technologies (IMAT) Program Overview
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you have a low-risk, high-reward project, there's probably a different mechanism that's better suited for you" | Speaker said: "We want you to pick the grant mechanism that works best for your project at your stage of development"
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO could provide a computational framework to predict performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, throughput) for emerging IMAT technologies, helping applicants define quantitative targets required by reviewers.

talk_title: Single‑Cell Genomics Technology Development (IMAT R33)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The first versions are always completely unusable by anyone but us" | Speaker said: "We have to think about the arc of the technology from early proof‑of‑principle to a robust, user‑friendly protocol"
mechanistic_blindspot: High protocol failure rate (50% failure, 16‑hour uninterrupted run) limiting reproducibility
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model protocol failure modes and suggest optimization pathways for high‑failure single‑cell assays.

talk_title: UCSC Xena – Visualizing Multi‑omic Cancer Data (ITCR U24)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you have data that you're interested in visualizing using the same exact visualizations for that big public data, we make that possible for you to do it with your own data, and it just stays on your computer" | Speaker said: "Usability is innovative – a tool that is usable is itself an innovation"
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can add AI‑driven biomarker prioritization modules to Xena, enabling users to quickly identify clinically actionable signatures within multi‑omic datasets.
companies_to_monitor: Broad Institute (for competing visualization platforms); Illumina (for data integration pipelines)

talk_title: Training opportunities within the NCI Intramural Research Program
NCI fellowship training opportunities
Career Development Awards (K series)
R50 program for research specialists

institution: University of Tennessee Health Sciences, Memphis
talk_title: MEDOC Consortium Overview
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We know that two people can have the same BMI, but have vastly different obesity-related cancer risk profiles based on their metabolic health." | Speaker said: "We are building a transdisciplinary consortium to address the gap in large, well-annotated human studies paired with mechanistic preclinical models."
crispro_top_description: Need for metabolic health–based biomarkers beyond BMI to stratify cancer risk.

institution: University of Utah, Huntsman Cancer Institute
talk_title: Metabolic Dysregulation Definition and Ceramide Risk Score
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We are developing and validating a ceramide‑based colorectal cancer risk score in prospective cohorts." | Speaker said: "We are testing the effect of different modalities of weight loss, including bariatric surgery and incretin mimetic therapy, on this risk score."
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential redundancy of ceramide pathways with other sphingolipid metabolites not captured in the score
crispro_top_description: DES1 inhibitor program lacks clinical validation; opportunity to repurpose with CrisPRO‑identified metabolic signatures.

talk_title: AFABP as Biomarker and Immunotherapy Target
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have developed multiple anti‑AFABP antibody for immunotherapy and have confirmed the therapeutic efficacy in multiple mouse models." | Speaker said: "We are evaluating AFABP as a circulating biomarker and potential target for immunotherapy."
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on normal adipose tissue leading to metabolic disturbances
crispro_top_description: Define circulating AFABP thresholds to select patients for anti‑AFABP therapy.

talk_title: Visceral Fat‑Induced Inflammation Inflammatype
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Our first goal is to come up with an inflammatype, which is essentially a molecular signature of proteins related to visceral fat‑induced inflammation." | Speaker said: "We will look at GI cancers, particularly colorectal and liver cancer, as low‑hanging fruit that we hope are related to visceral fat and induced inflammation."
mechanistic_blindspot: Inflammatory protein panel may miss non‑protein metabolic mediators (e.g., lipid mediators) driving cancer risk
crispro_top_description: Integrate lipidomic and metabolomic data with inflammatype to capture missing pathways.

talk_title: MEDOC Coordinating Center Infrastructure
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Our shared infrastructure includes harmonization beyond BMI to in‑depth obesity phenotyping and a common analytics backbone for predictive modeling." | Speaker said: "We have a biorepository with hundreds of thousands of relationships in a graph database."
crispro_top_description: Leverage MEDOC’s harmonized phenotyping to embed CrisPRO‑derived metabolic signatures into upcoming prevention trials.

institution: National Cancer Institute, Biorepositories and Biospecimen Research Branch
talk_title: Biospecimen Science Funding Opportunities
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we didn't have any funding to do any pre-analytics" | Speaker noted: "the funding opportunity kind of fills the gap"
crispro_top_description: Researchers need pre-analytical standardization for biomarker assays, which is not addressed by existing funding mechanisms.

institution: National Cancer Institute, Cancer Diagnosis Program
talk_title: UH2/UH3 Funding for Biomarker Assay Validation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker emphasized: "the goal of the program is not regulatory approval, but assay readiness for clinical use" | Speaker warned: "applications that propose early stage development of technologies... would be more appropriate for other opportunities"
crispro_top_description: The program seeks assays that can monitor treatment resistance; CrisPRO can model resistance pathways to guide assay design.

talk_title: Tumor Immune Microenvironment: Time to Chat
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I think this is really the beginning of an iceberg." | Speaker said: "We are beginning to think that there are very specific synapses with its target cells." | Speaker said: "We have to be able to target these in a very organ-specific manner, and we're working very hard on it."
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of understanding of organ‑specific acetylcholine receptor subtypes and their downstream effects
crispro_top_description: Identify specific acetylcholine receptor subtypes (muscarinic/nicotinic) that correlate with tumor prognosis across organ sites.

talk_title: Profiling and Targeting Innervating Peripheral Neurons in Pancreatic Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "It was not rocket science to actually combine the two." | Speaker said: "Denervation may turn immunological cold pancreatic cancer into a checkpoint inhibitor responsive hot tumor." | Speaker said: "We are currently trying to generate a neuro‑pan‑cancer atlas."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed dense neuronal sprouting in PDAC tumors (observation) but concluded that targeting the entire peripheral nervous system will dramatically halt tumor growth (contradiction without addressing potential compensatory pathways).
mechanistic_blindspot: Unclear contribution of nerve‑targeting versus cytotoxic effects on tumor cells
crispro_top_description: Incorporate baseline neural imaging biomarkers to stratify patients and enrich for those with high tumor innervation.
companies_to_monitor: Revolution Medicines; Companies developing peripheral nerve modulators

talk_title: Nociceptor Neurons Drive CD8 T‑Cell Exhaustion via CGRP in Melanoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We found that CGRP seems to drive the exhaustion of those T cells." | Speaker said: "High RAMP1 expression melanoma seems to be bad for the patient." | Speaker said: "Nerve injury signature correlates with poor prognosis across cancer types."
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that melanoma cells up‑regulate RAMP1 in lung metastases (observation) but concluded that RAMP1 antagonists could be broadly effective without addressing potential tissue‑specific effects.
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of therapeutic agents that selectively block tumor‑derived CGRP without affecting normal sensory function
crispro_top_description: Repurpose existing migraine RAMP1 antagonists for melanoma by defining a biomarker‑driven patient subset with high tumor RAMP1 expression.
cited_competitors: Migraine drug manufacturers (e.g., Amgen, Eli Lilly)
companies_to_monitor: Pharma companies developing CGRP pathway inhibitors

talk_title: Biparatopic antibodies targeting FGFR2 fusions and ADC development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see striking absence of FGFR2 on the surface" | Speaker said: "we think the activity was primarily driven by loss of FGFR2 through internalization and degradation" | Speaker said: "the small molecule inhibitor gave about -20% tumor regression, not as good as the small molecule, but it's also not so far off" | Speaker said: "we got pretty good degradation without having to do any additional manipulation of the antibody"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that biparatopic antibodies cause robust FGFR2 internalization and degradation (mechanistic observation) but concluded that the antibodies are "not as good as the small molecule" (contradictory efficacy conclusion)
failing_target: Biparatopic FGFR2 antibodies (e.g., BD, BC) as monotherapy
mechanistic_blindspot: Inability to fully regress tumors despite internalization; unclear why some biparatopic formats become agonists in ligand‑independent settings
crispro_top_description: FGFR2 fusion status and kinase‑domain mutation status are not used to select patients in the preclinical models, representing a gap for CrisPRO predictive biomarker integration.

talk_title: Zenitatamab (zanidatamab) – a biparatopic HER2 antibody and trispecific costimulatory antibodies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Zani mediates very potent CDC, whereas the individual antibodies and the combination did not" | Speaker said: "zenatuximab meets full tumor regression at closely equivalent or even lesser dosing" | Speaker said: "the progression‑free survival and overall survival benefit was observed independent of PD‑L1 status" | Speaker said: "the dual scFv format showed loss of growth inhibitory potential and potential agonism"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that zenitatamab creates large HER2 clusters enabling CDC (scientific observation) but also stated that the antibody’s overall antitumor activity is comparable to the trastuzumab + pertuzumab combo in many settings (contradictory conclusion) | Speaker demonstrated that trispecifics can provide superior T‑cell activation (observation) yet admitted that most trispecifics in the clinic have shown limited antitumor activity (contradiction)
mechanistic_blindspot: Spatial distance between antigen‑binding domains leading to unintended HER2 agonism
crispro_top_description: Identify HER2 expression patterns or co‑mutations that predict the CDC‑mediated advantage of zenitatamab over trastuzumab + pertuzumab.
nct_numbers: NCT04512345  // hypothetical zenitatamab phase III biliary tract trial; NCT04678901  // hypothetical zenitatamab + tislelizumab triplet trial

talk_title: Two‑plus‑one (2+1) bispecific T‑cell engagers and CD28 co‑stimulatory strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the two plus one format provides avidity‑driven selectivity" | Speaker said: "we observed a ~70% CRS rate during priming, but low grade‑3 and no grade‑4 events at target dose" | Speaker said: "the strongest progressors had absolutely no EMPP3 expression" | Speaker said: "we design FC‑silenced versions to avoid non‑selective T‑cell activation"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust tumor regression in EMPP3‑high RCC patients (observation) but also highlighted that the two best responders lacked EMPP3 expression (contradiction), suggesting an unclear mechanism of action
mechanistic_blindspot: Efficacy observed in patients lacking EMPP3 expression suggests off‑target or bystander effects not fully understood
crispro_top_description: Develop a robust EMPP3 IHC/flow cytometry assay to enrich for responders and reduce exposure of EMPP3‑negative patients.
cited_competitors: Amgen AMG 509 (2+1 FGFR2 T‑cell engager); Zencor EMPP3 2+1 program
nct_numbers: NCT05812345  // hypothetical Zencor EMPP3 2+1 Phase 1 trial

talk_title: Development of NEO811, a molecular glue degrader of ARNT, which is also HIF-1 beta, with potent single activity in clear cell renal cell carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't expect that our mechanism will avoid the well-established consequence such as anemia, but we believe that these are easily monitorable and manageable. | Speaker said: we have not disclosed all the biomarker strategy data at this time.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed Cryo‑EM data indicating a complex, multi‑domain induced degron, yet concluded that the approach will be broadly combinable with all downstream HIF inhibitors without addressing potential pathway cross‑talk.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential redundancy of HIF-2α inhibition in tumors with low HIF-2α expression
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can model the pan‑HIF degron interface to predict and design next‑generation glues that retain activity in HIF‑2α low tumors.

talk_title: AZD8359, a novel CD8-based T-cell engager designed to deliver a wide therapeutic window in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't see any detrimental effect, even in durability, in all sorts of aspects of this reduced engagement of the CD4s. | Speaker said: we have not had any red flags in anything we've been doing that suggests that biasing to the CD8s is increasing our ADA risk.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed in vitro data that CD4 T cells produce cytokines and contribute to antitumor activity, yet concluded that biasing to CD8 will reduce CRS without compromising efficacy.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential loss of CD4 helper function leading to reduced long‑term T‑cell memory
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can develop a predictive model for STEAP2 expression thresholds needed for optimal AZD8359 activity.

talk_title: TNG961, a selective oral molecular glue degrader of HBS1L for the treatment of FOCAD deficient cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we have a favorable safety profile for TNG961 and achieved exposures 20‑fold above our human predicted efficacious dose. | Speaker said: we anticipate clinical activity as monotherapy, as well as we have observed combination benefit with PRMT5 inhibitors.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented proteomics showing exclusive HBS1L degradation, yet later admitted off‑target GSPT1 activity appears at higher concentrations.
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target degradation of GSPT1 at higher concentrations
crispro_top_description: CrisPRO can integrate FOCAD, MTAP, and SKI complex genomic data to define a high‑confidence patient cohort for TNG961.

talk_title: EPI-326, a novel mutation‑independent and normal tissue‑sparing EGFR bispecific antibody that degrades EGFR for the treatment of EGFR‑driven solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we saw no EGFR‑related toxicities nor any toxicities at the maximal feasible dose of 204 mg/kg in non‑human primates. | Speaker said: the EGFR binder maintains normal signaling in skin keratinocytes, unlike current EGFR blockers.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that EGFR blockade alone only yields tumor stasis, yet concluded that degradation will be curative across all EGFR‑driven tumors without presenting data in EGFR‑independent contexts.
mechanistic_blindspot: Tumors lacking EGFR dependence may not respond despite EGFR degradation
crispro_top_description: Use CrisPRO to model integrin β6 expression thresholds needed for effective EGFR degradation.

talk_title: First‑in‑class B7H3‑targeted sialidase for boosting innate and adaptive anti‑tumor immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we only saw a modest clinical activity and only in the hypersialylated patients" | Speaker said: "we felt that this may be insufficient to sustain immune activation" | Speaker said: "the desialylation was only lasting a couple of days on tumors"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed paired biopsies with 80‑90% reduction of CD163+ M2 macrophages (strong mechanistic effect) but concluded that single‑agent activity was modest and insufficient to move forward
mechanistic_blindspot: Transient tumor desialylation lasting only 2–3 days, limiting sustained immune activation
crispro_top_description: Lack of prospective selection of patients with high tumor hypersialylation limits signal detection

talk_title: ECI‑830, a potent and highly selective CDK2 inhibitor for HR‑positive/HER2‑negative breast cancer and CCNE1‑amplified tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we observed a shift when you go from biochemical to cell… still very potent" | Speaker said: "the addition of ECI‑830 to ribociclib further suppresses phosphorylated Rb"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented crystal structure showing high selectivity for CDK2, yet acknowledged that CDK2 inhibition alone has limited single‑agent activity in HR‑positive breast cancer
failing_target: ECI‑830 monotherapy in HR‑positive breast cancer
mechanistic_blindspot: CDK2 inhibition alone may not fully suppress Rb phosphorylation in RB‑intact tumors
crispro_top_description: Need for robust CCNE1 amplification or cyclin E expression assay to enrich for responders

talk_title: TRI‑611, a potent selective CNS‑penetrant ALK molecular glue degrader for ALK‑fusion positive NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "TRI‑611 is remarkably clean" | Speaker said: "TRI‑611 induces degradation of full‑length endogenous EML4‑ALK" | Speaker said: "TRI‑611 is active against all of the mutations that we have put into this system"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TRI‑611 degrades ALK in vitro and in vivo, yet concluded that the safety profile is “remarkably clean” without presenting any preclinical toxicity data
mechanistic_blindspot: Resistance mutations cluster in the ATP‑binding pocket, leaving distal degrader site unexploited
crispro_top_description: TRI‑611 can be positioned to replace failed ALK TKIs in patients with resistance mutations

talk_title: Discovery and development of a CD30 dual‑payload ADC combining MMAE and TOP1 inhibition
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "our dual payload ADC was tolerated at 60 mg/kg, far above the 10 mg/kg dose of brentuximab vedotin" | Speaker said: "the dual payload ADC showed robust tumor regression at a sub‑optimal dose where single‑payload ADCs did not" | Speaker said: "the chemistry is highly stable, resulting in very little payload loss"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that the dual‑payload ADC has markedly improved tolerability (MTD 60 mg/kg) yet attributed the safety gain primarily to the stable chemistry rather than the reduced MMAE DAR, despite presenting data showing lower MMAE load
mechanistic_blindspot: Efflux of MMAE via P‑gp reduces intracellular toxin concentration
crispro_top_description: Identify synergistic agents that further overcome P‑gp mediated resistance when combined with the dual‑payload ADC

talk_title: CID-078, a first‑in‑class oral macrocycle Cyclin AB RXL inhibitor
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we often get asked how you're able to obtain selectivity over the other cyclins, given the hydrophobic patch is so highly conserved" | Speaker noted: "we have a Phase I trial ongoing now"
crispro_top_description: RB loss and E2F‑high status are proposed biomarkers but were not incorporated into a prospective trial design; CrisPRO could develop a predictive model to prospectively select patients

talk_title: AMX883, an oral BRD9 degrader for AML
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we saw no emergence of resistance to venetoclax when cultured with venetoclax and AMX883" | Speaker noted: "we anticipate this being in the clinic in the second half of this year"
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential cardiac QT liability observed with earlier BRD9 PROTACs, not fully addressed for AMX883
crispro_top_description: BRD9 dependency is inferred from AML subtype but not prospectively validated; CrisPRO could develop a transcriptomic signature of non‑canonical BAF reliance

talk_title: JNJ‑2175, an ENPP3‑targeted ADC for solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see broad and high ENPP3 expression across several solid tumors" | Speaker noted: "the molecule is in Phase 1 dose‑escalation study"
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential heme‑related toxicity from basophil activation not experimentally addressed
crispro_top_description: ENPP3 IHC positivity alone may not predict ADC delivery; CrisPRO could integrate tumor microenvironment features to refine patient selection

talk_title: IPN‑1203, a dual Vβ6/Vβ10 T‑cell agonist with IL‑15 co‑stimulation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see a marked reduction in exhausted cell population, more than 50%" | Speaker noted: "we are now in phase one"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed scRNA‑seq data indicating strong central‑memory imprinting, yet concluded that "minimal cytokine release" will ensure safety, ignoring the potential for delayed cytokine storm from expanded memory T cells
mechanistic_blindspot: Patients lacking sufficient Vβ6/Vβ10 TCR repertoire may not respond, but no pre‑screening strategy discussed
crispro_top_description: Develop a CrisPRO model to predict patient Vβ6/Vβ10 repertoire size from peripheral blood sequencing and select responders

talk_title: Safety and efficacy of elacrasib, a next‑generation GDP‑bound KRAS G12C inhibitor in advanced NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the study may underestimate the actual risk of autoimmune hepatitis, given that both of these would reduce the frequency of this event in this population." | Speaker noted: "this raises the question of how many of these responses are true circumvention of an acquired resistance mechanism versus just re‑response in a patient where sensitive clones have grown out again after a wash‑out period." | Speaker presented: "median OS was not mature, with a 12‑month OS rate being 68%" but did not provide any OS hazard ratio or longer follow‑up.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed deep and durable radiologic responses (e.g., 73% ORR in colorectal) yet simultaneously admitted that the study may underestimate autoimmune hepatitis risk and that many refractory responses could be due to drug wash‑out rather than true resistance overcoming
mechanistic_blindspot: bypass signaling via KRAS amplification and other RTK‑driven pathways not fully suppressed by GDP‑state inhibition
crispro_top_description: Baseline KRAS G12C ctDNA positivity strongly predicts response, yet the trial did not require ctDNA screening

talk_title: Landscape of KRAS G12C off‑inhibitors and future directions
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the reality is that the modest clinical activity of existing KRAS G12C off inhibitor monotherapy has led to a rapid push to develop combination strategies" | Speaker admitted: "I unfortunately will have to disappoint you – the only thing we can say at this point is that it's still anyone's game"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented modest response rates (e.g., 28% for sotorasib) yet concluded that KRAS G12C off‑inhibitors have "revolutionized the field" and are now standard of care
mechanistic_blindspot: Compensatory pathway activation keeping KRAS in GTP‑bound state (e.g., RTK up‑regulation, KRAS amplification)
crispro_top_description: Targeting the GTP‑bound KRAS state (on‑inhibitors) could overcome bypass activation that limits off‑inhibitors

institution: University of California Davis Comprehensive Cancer Center
talk_title: Preliminary safety and activity of zoldonrasib (RMC‑9805), a KRAS G12D on‑state tri‑complex inhibitor
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the study may underestimate the actual risk of autoimmune hepatitis, given that both of these would reduce the frequency of this event in this population" | Speaker admitted: "we don't yet have data on ERK as a resistance node in this trial"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented deep and durable responses (median PFS 11.1 mo) while simultaneously noting that the trial may underestimate autoimmune hepatitis risk and that resistance mechanisms such as ERK activation remain uncharacterized
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential emergence of KRAS amplification or alternative KRAS isoforms not covered by G12D‑specific binding
crispro_top_description: Baseline KRAS G12D ctDNA detection predicts higher response but was not required for enrollment

talk_title: Phase I trial of WEE1 inhibitor zetaracib + PKMYT1 inhibitor lonresorib in CCNE1/FBXW7/PPP2R1A altered tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the combination was very well‑tolerated" despite targeting two cell‑cycle kinases | Speaker noted: "no clinically relevant myelosuppression events were observed as a safety concern"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized a strong safety profile (no myelosuppression) while presenting modest efficacy (10% ORR) in a heavily pre‑treated, biomarker‑selected population
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential redundancy via MYT1 overexpression or alternative CDK1 regulators not inhibited by the combo
crispro_top_description: Distinguish CCNE1 amplification from overexpression to enrich for truly replication‑stress dependent tumors

institution: Circle Pharma / University Hospital Seidman Cancer Center
talk_title: Phase I evaluation of CID‑078, a novel cyclin A/B RxR inhibitor
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "CID‑078 has been well‑tolerated. Minimal grade three alterations have been seen" | Speaker noted: "no clear signal of dose relatedness has been seen" despite exploring a wide dose range
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented impressive preclinical potency and two individual patient responses while simultaneously acknowledging that pharmacodynamic target engagement data are still pending
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential lack of activity in tumors without RB pathway loss; reliance on RB loss may limit patient pool
crispro_top_description: Standardize RB loss detection via immunohistochemistry rather than genomic sequencing to enrich for responsive patients

talk_title: Representation learning over cancer genomes, scaling petabytes to predictions
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "It's important to recognize that identifying the cause of something is different from predicting an outcome." | Speaker said: "We think that the HR Difuse model itself ... does perform better than both these models by a fair margin." | Speaker said: "We are a bit shocked by how well we're able to do here... approaching 0.75 in terms of F1."
crispro_top_description: HRDfuse improves HRD detection in non‑BRCA associated cancers where existing tools (HRDetect, CORG) underperform.

talk_title: Dimensional structural chromosome instability in human cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We think HSRs are actually really interesting because they're good news..." | Speaker said: "We call this method JAVEL ... state‑of‑the‑art approach that takes into account rearrangements and copy number as input and outputs what I think is a state‑of‑the‑art copy number profiles." | Speaker said: "We estimate about 30 dimensions of structural SIN across thousands of cancers, and some of these may provide insight into targetable synthetic lethalities detectable only with whole‑genome sequencing."
crispro_top_description: Structural SIN signatures (e.g., BFB, Typhonis) correlate with synthetic lethal vulnerabilities, offering combination therapy targets.

institution: Mass General Hospital and Broad Institute
talk_title: Systematic analysis of >8,000 TCGA whole genomes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There is a new sequencing artifact ... we call that a vapor trail and we developed a tool to filter it." | Speaker said: "We find many more mutations in GC‑rich regions that were missed by the older PEACOG data." | Speaker said: "We identified novel non‑coding drivers and structural variation drivers, some of which are completely new."
mechanistic_blindspot: 30‑50X coverage missed mutations in GC‑rich promoters and first exons
crispro_top_description: Re‑process PEACOG samples with PCR‑free 80X WGS to recover missed driver mutations for previously failed biomarker programs.

talk_title: Spatial metabolomics of pancreas and breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the trial was never powered to give a definitive answer, right, but it had 16 valuable patients per arm randomized" | Speaker said: "When they were in ketosis, they were not in very deep ketosis, and there was a correlation between the extent of ketosis and longer survival in the trial"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that glucose is depleted in cancer cells (spatial metabolomics) yet concluded that further glucose restriction via ketogenic diet could be therapeutically beneficial – ignoring the paradox that cancer cells already appear glucose‑starved
failing_target: Ketogenic diet + standard chemotherapy in metastatic pancreatic cancer
mechanistic_blindspot: Trial did not control for phytochemical differences between standard chow and ketogenic diet, confounding interpretation of efficacy
crispro_top_description: Identify patients with high intratumoral ketone utilization (e.g., CD8+ T‑cell 3‑hydroxybutyrate uptake) to enrich for responders to ketogenic diet‑based regimens

talk_title: Metabolic regulation of cancer cell growth via mitochondrial NADPH
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We observed that loss of NDK2 completely disrupted proline biosynthesis" | Speaker said: "We see a reduction in lipoic acid in all of these proteins, indicating that NADK2 loss affects general protein lipoylation"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that mitochondrial NADPH supports TCA cycle flux via PDH lipoylation, yet concluded that the main function of mitochondrial NADPH is proline synthesis – overlooking the broader metabolic impact
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory pathways for mitochondrial NADPH (e.g., cytosolic NADK retargeted to mitochondria) not fully explored
crispro_top_description: Target the proline auxotrophy induced by NDK2 loss with proline‑degrading enzymes or transport inhibitors to prevent rescue of tumor cells
cited_competitors: Blenis and Anaguma labs; Ralph DeBerardinis

talk_title: Methodological insights into whole‑body metabolism and nucleotide constraints in cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you simply take the folate level in MPM and make it look like RPMI, now they don't care about nucleotide salvage at all" | Speaker said: "Standard mouse chow is actually high in folates because we breed the mice"
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that folate depletion dramatically slows B‑ALL growth, yet suggested that standard culture conditions are “physiological” for nucleotide metabolism – ignoring the mismatch
failing_target: Low extracellular folate in physiological plasma‑like media
mechanistic_blindspot: Folates are the limiting cofactor for de novo nucleotide synthesis; standard culture media overestimates folate availability
crispro_top_description: Develop a folate‑status biomarker panel to identify tumors reliant on salvage pathways for nucleotide synthesis
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing plasma‑like media platforms for drug screening

talk_title: Extracellular Vesicles and Particles, Their Biogenesis and Function
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't know why some recipient cells are more susceptible to cargo uptake | Speaker said: we don't fully understand the mechanisms of microvesicle uptake
mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of identified receptors on recipient cells that mediate selective uptake of TMVs
crispro_top_description: Identify and target the unknown recipient cell receptors that mediate TMV uptake to block pro‑invasive signaling.

institution: Los Angeles and Sapienza University, Rome
talk_title: Biology to Translation of Large Oncosomes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we cannot be sure that isolated EVs are tumor‑derived | Speaker said: we are still working on robust clinical‑grade enrichment methods
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that large oncosomes strongly promote metastasis in mice, then concluded that current EV‑based diagnostics are ready for clinical use despite acknowledging contamination and enrichment challenges
mechanistic_blindspot: Potential contamination with non‑tumor EVs and lipoproteins despite size‑based separation
crispro_top_description: Create a CrisPRO‑driven multi‑analyte panel focusing on mitochondrial transcripts and palmitoylated proteins enriched in large oncosomes.
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing EV‑based liquid biopsy platforms

talk_title: Activated T‑cell Derived Extracellular Vesicles as Immunomodulatory Therapeutics
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't know why some tumors respond to T‑cell EVs | Speaker said: we are still figuring out the exact mechanism of DNA packaging
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust anti‑tumor effects of activated T‑cell EVs, yet admitted that the underlying DNA‑mediated mechanism is still unknown
failing_target: Activated T‑cell EV DNA packaging pathway
mechanistic_blindspot: Unidentified genes governing EV DNA export (immune developmental genes only partially known)
crispro_top_description: Use CrisPRO to identify and engineer T‑cell EVs with optimized DNA cargo that maximizes antigen presentation without triggering autoimmunity.

institution: University of California, Davis / Luminance Biosciences
talk_title: Integrin αvβ6-targeted radioligand therapy development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We saw more lesions with our F-18 peptide than with FDG-PET, but the patient already knew they had metastatic disease. So we asked ourselves what can we do with this information to actually help a patient?" | Speaker noted: "No SAEs, no adverse events" despite single dose administration.
cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high‑resolution PET images detecting hundreds of lesions, then concluded that the agent "didn't help the patient" because the disease was already known, contradicting the diagnostic value of the imaging
mechanistic_blindspot: Limited tumor penetration and rapid renal clearance may reduce delivered dose to deep tumor sites
crispro_top_description: Develop a quantitative αvβ6 PET‑based cutoff to select patients likely to receive therapeutic dose

talk_title: DLL3‑targeted radiopharmaceuticals and theranostics
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We can inject small amounts of radiolabeled antibody – three milligrams total – and still get clear imaging" | Speaker noted: "The failed ADC trial (Rova‑T) was stopped because of toxicity, not because the antibody was bad"
